issue_title: Why does flower require exactly `tornado==4.2.0`?
issue_body:
Instead of `tornado>=4.2.0`? This seems very restrictive. Flower doesn't know what project it will be installed into and what packages might be installed alongside it, and they might require `4.3` (which probably also works with Flower)?

This commit relaxed all the other requirements except for tornado: https://github.com/mher/flower/commit/57e87e184edbd751b6a2ab88a886ba41e2755e0f

issue_title: txzmq.ZmqREQConnection messageReceived code is returning weird results
issue_body:
When I call the following code:

```
def prime_data(msg):
	print( msg )

poll = ZmqREQConnection(self.factory, ZmqEndpoint("connect", "127.0.0.1:5001))
poll.messageReceived = prime_data
poll.sendMsg(b"*")
```

I get the following output:
>  [b'\x7fs\x8d\xf1\xfa\x19G\x08\xbd\xff\x91\xcf\xcb\x14KB', b'', b'ACTUAL CONTENT OF MESSAGE...']

Comparing `ZmqREPConnection`'s and `ZmqREQConnection`'s implementations of `messageReceived` methods [here](https://github.com/smira/txZMQ/blob/master/txzmq/req_rep.py#L171) and [here](https://github.com/smira/txZMQ/blob/master/txzmq/req_rep.py#L124), I'm wondering if there isn't a missing step for `ZmqREQConnection`'s processing of incoming messages.

Perhaps I'm missing something obvious? This isn't exactly show stopper bug, but it does seems like an inconsistent API.

issue_title: Crash "pew wipeenv venv" like "pew rm venv"
issue_body:
Please consider a bash script in "strict mode" (*)

```
set -euo pipefail
IFS=$'\n\t'

VENV_NAME=venv

pew wipeenv $VENV_NAME
pew rm      $VENV_NAME
pew new -d  $VENV_NAME
pew in      $VENV_NAME pip install --upgrade blahblah
```

If the venv does not exist the script will show
`
ERROR: Environment 'venv' does not exist.
`
and exit.

If we comment out the line with wipeenv and run the script (with not existing venv), the script will NOT crash at rm, the result will be:
`
Error while trying to remove the d:\Users\myuser\.local\share\virtualenvs\venv env:
The system cannot find the path specified
`
but the bash script will continue. 

I think that the reason is that wipenv returns a different error code from rm or otherwise crashes in less pleasant way.

I suggest that wipeenv crashes in the same way as rm, for consistency.

I checked the above on Windows 10 with Git's GNU bash, version 3.1.23(6), no access to Linux today but probably it would work in the same way.

(*)
http://redsymbol.net/articles/unofficial-bash-strict-mode/

issue_title: Something wrong with [char-class]{quantity}
issue_body:
I am seeing different counts than expected when using braced fixed-quantities after character class expressions.  There is a regex for the numeric range 0 to 65535, whose count should be 65536:

```
>>> exrex.count('^([0-9]|[1-9][0-9]|[1-9][0-9][0-9]|[1-9][0-9][0-9][0-9]|[1-5][0-9][0-9][0-9][0-9]|6[0-4][0-9][0-9][0-9]|65[0-4][0-9][0-9]|655[0-2][0-9]|6553[0-5])$')                
65536
```

While count() works great in the above case, it does not when I condense the regex using braced fixed-quantities:
```
>>> exrex.count('^([0-9]|[1-9][0-9]|[1-9][0-9]{2}|[1-9][0-9]{3}]|[1-5][0-9]{4}|6[0-4][0-9]{3}|65[0-4][0-9]{2}|655[0-2][0-9]|6553[0-5])$')
12369
```

To make it more interesting, the braced fixed quantities work again it they are grouped using parentheses with the expression they are repeating:

```
>>> exrex.count('^([0-9]|[1-9][0-9]|[1-9]([0-9]{2})|[1-9]([0-9]{3})]|[1-5]([0-9]{4})|6[0-4]([0-9]{3})|65[0-4]([0-9]{2})|655[0-2][0-9]|6553[0-5])$')
65536
```

So, there appears to be a bug somewhere in what gets generated when the regex includes quantities for character classes and using grouping to ensure the quantity applies to just the preceding character class appears to address it.  Thanks!

from astropy import units as u

Thanks to C. Monstein for pointing this out.

issue_title: XQuartz and Anaconda Python building and library linking errors
issue_body:
tl;dr: I fixed errors building dlib by disabling the GUI, and fixed errors linking to libmkl_rt.dylib by modifying the .so with `install_name_change`.

I'm using OSX 10.11 with XCode 8.2.1 and Anaconda. I have CUDA 8.0.55 and CuDNN 5.1.10.

```
$ brew cask install Caskroom/cask/xquartz
$ brew install boost boost-python
$ mkdir build; cd build; cmake .. ; cmake --build .
```

CUDA and CuDNN are successfully detected + built by `cmake ..`. But `cmake --build .` gives me an error pretty early on:

```
[  1%] Building CXX object dlib/CMakeFiles/dlib.dir/gui_widgets/fonts.cpp.o
In file included from /Users/kyle/dlib/dlib/gui_widgets/fonts.cpp:14:
/Users/kyle/dlib/dlib/gui_widgets/nativefont.h:313:21: error: use of undeclared identifier 'XAllocColor'
```

Instead of digging into this, I just disabled the GUI:

```
$ cmake .. -DDLIB_NO_GUI_SUPPORT:BOOL=ON
$ cmake --build .
```

And it build dlib successfully. Time to build Python support (specifying no GUI here too):

```
$ cd .. ; python setup.py install --yes DLIB_NO_GUI_SUPPORT
```

Success! But then...

```
$ python
kyle@kyle ~/D/L/dlib (master)> python
Python 2.7.12 |Anaconda 4.0.0 (x86_64)| (default, Jul  2 2016, 17:43:17) 
[GCC 4.2.1 (Based on Apple Inc. build 5658) (LLVM build 2336.11.00)] on darwin
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
Anaconda is brought to you by Continuum Analytics.
Please check out: http://continuum.io/thanks and https://anaconda.org
>>> import dlib
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/Users/kyle/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/dlib-19.2.99-py2.7-macosx-10.6-x86_64.egg/dlib/__init__.py", line 1, in <module>
    from .dlib import *
ImportError: dlopen(/Users/kyle/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/dlib-19.2.99-py2.7-macosx-10.6-x86_64.egg/dlib/dlib.so, 2): Library not loaded: libmkl_rt.dylib
  Referenced from: /Users/kyle/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/dlib-19.2.99-py2.7-macosx-10.6-x86_64.egg/dlib/dlib.so
  Reason: image not found
```

Tried to learn more about this:

```
$ otool -L ~/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/dlib-19.2.99-py2.7-macosx-10.6-x86_64.egg/dlib/dlib.so | grep mkl
	libmkl_rt.dylib (compatibility version 0.0.0, current version 0.0.0)
```

To me this looks like it should have an `@rpath` at the beginning? So I do this:

```
$ install_name_change -change libmkl_rt.dylib @rpath/libmkl_rt.dylib ~/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/dlib-19.2.99-py2.7-macosx-10.6-x86_64.egg/dlib/dlib.so
$ python
>> import dlib
>>
```

Success! Hopefully... maybe I just delayed the inevitable, and it was linked to another dylib? Then I go to run the Python examples:

```
$ cd ../python_examples ; python face_recognition.py
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "face_recognition.py", line 50, in <module>
    import dlib
ImportError: dlopen(/Users/kyle/Documents/Learning/dlib/python_examples/dlib.so, 2): Library not loaded: libmkl_rt.dylib
  Referenced from: /Users/kyle/Documents/Learning/dlib/python_examples/dlib.so
```

Looks like there's another copy of `dlib.so` here. I suppose I could just delete it, or make the same change. I opt to make the same change:

```
$ install_name_change -change libmkl_rt.dylib @rpath/libmkl_rt.dylib dlib.so
$ python face_recognition.py 
Call this program like this:
   ./face_recognition.py shape_predictor_68_face_landmarks.dat dlib_face_recognition_resnet_model_v1.dat ../examples/faces
You can download a trained facial shape predictor and recognition model from:
    http://dlib.net/files/shape_predictor_68_face_landmarks.dat.bz2
    http://dlib.net/files/dlib_face_recognition_resnet_model_v1.dat.bz2
```

Getting closer...

```
$ curl -O http://dlib.net/files/shape_predictor_68_face_landmarks.dat.bz2
$ curl -O http://dlib.net/files/dlib_face_recognition_resnet_model_v1.dat.bz2
$ bunzip2 shape_predictor_68_face_landmarks.dat.bz2
$ bunzip2 dlib_face_recognition_resnet_model_v1.dat.bz2
$ python face_recognition.py shape_predictor_68_face_landmarks.dat dlib_face_recognition_resnet_model_v1.dat ../examples/faces
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "face_recognition.py", line 74, in <module>
    win = dlib.image_window()
AttributeError: 'module' object has no attribute 'image_window'
```

Right, I didn't install the GUI components. I comment out all the lines referencing the `win` variable, and:

```
$ python face_recognition.py shape_predictor_68_face_landmarks.dat dlib_face_recognition_resnet_model_v1.dat ../examples/faces
Processing file: ../examples/faces/2007_007763.jpg
Number of faces detected: 7
Detection 0: Left: 93 Top: 194 Right: 129 Bottom: 230
-0.0512203
0.0150111
0.0642802
-0.0438789
...
```

Success!

Overall I'm posting this issue to ask a couple questions:

1. Should I have done something differently with XQuartz at the beginning?
2. Is there a variable I should set in CMake to work with Anaconda correctly? I saw in another issue you recommend just disabling Anaconda completely for building and running dlib. Is this still the recommendation, or is there another way?

issue_title: setCudeDevice issue
locate_point() uses start/end incorrectly
issue_body:
This is identical to #42 but for curves instead of surfaces.

The failing test added in 9308b0e185cf505dddc7dfa792640ce9057c0ad8 showcases the problem. Adding an equivalent breakpoint at

https://github.com/dhermes/bezier/blob/9308b0e185cf505dddc7dfa792640ce9057c0ad8/src/bezier/_curve_helpers.py#L784

we see

```python
(Pdb) s_approx
0.625
(Pdb) newton_refine(curve._nodes, curve._degree, point, s_approx)
0.51249999999999996
```

Note that `0.625` corresponds to the midpoint of the shifted interval: `0.5 * (0.25 + 1.0) == 0.625`

issue_title: pull_parents doesn't look at adds from other containers
issue_body:
It's possible to have a harpoon.yml like the following:

    ---
    images:
      my_amazing_image:
        commands:
          - FROM centos:7
          - - ADD
            - dest: /
              mtime: 1464736365
              content:
                image: "some_image_with_firefox"
                path: /firefox.tar.bz2

In this configuration it will add the contents of `/firefox.tar.gz2` from the `some_image_with_firefox` image to the root of the `my_amazing_image`.

I would expect that when we do a `harpoon pull_parents` that it tries to pull down `some_image_with_firefox` so that it is available to copy things from it.

issue_title: get_code() fidelity
issue_body:
I started this discussion via email, so we start right in the middle :-)

I want to write a re-factoring based on AST transformation. So it is important that get_code() will reproduce original formatting 100% accurate, or people will hate the re-factoring.

I noticed that get_code() is quite sloppy, so we agreed that I rewrite get_code. It will use the start_pos and token_lists to recreate the code. I also noticed that not everything we need has a token_list and start_position. For example docstrings are missing it. We already agreed that I add this functionality. So here is my proposal:

Could we make string literals a representation object? So we can place it in the docstring member. I would implement ```__str__()``` so we don't break existing code. The string literal object would have a start_position and maybe some other meta information like line-count. I would even place it in the token_lists (if it doesn't break too much). At the moment string literals are just plain token-tuples. (see below)

```python
    
    code_basic_features = '''
    def a_function(a_argument, a_default = "default"):
        """A docstring"""
    
        a_string = """What about this?
    <- This I mean"""
        a_result = 3 * a_argument
        print(a_result)  # a comment
        if a_default == "default":
            return str(a_result)
        else:
            return None
    '''
    prs = parser.Parser(code_basic_features)
    scope1 = prs.top_module.subscopes[0]
    freeze.freeze(scope1.statements[0])

->  (('docstr', ''),
     ('_assignment_details', ()),
     ('token_list',
      ((('names',
         ((('parent', 'seen: <Name: a_string@5,4>'), ('_start_pos', (5, 4))),)),),
       (52, '=', (5, 13)),
       (3, '"""What about this?\n<- This I mean"""', (5, 15)))),
     ('_set_vars', None),
     ('as_names', ()),
     ('_names_are_set_vars', False),
     ('_commands', None))
```

issue_title: process.create_time() and hash(process) are not immutable on linux
issue_body:
I've run into two related issues using psutil on linux (Ubuntu 14.04.3):
1. Because of the way Process.create_time() is calculated it changes if the system time changes (commonly from ntp corrections, but in my instance I noticed it because the clock changed during the boot sequence).  This may or may not be a big deal depending on what you want the creation time for.
2. The documentation states that hash(process) is calculated from the pid and the creation_time, thus if the creation_time changes the hash also changes.  This is a big deal since it defeats the point of a hash.

The problem does not appear to be present on Windows, presumably due to the way creation_time is calculated.

To reproduce:
1) Pick a process, e.g. 12345
2) Start python from the command line (Note: the actual values are just for example purposes)
```python
import psutil
p = psutil.Process(12345)
p.creation_time()
>>>1447426717.05
hash(p)
>>>10728053
```
3) Change the date: date -s "Fri Nov 13 10:09:44 EST 2015"
4) Start python again (Note: it is important to restart python since psutil caches the value BASE_TIME which it uses to calculate create_time)
```python
import psutil
p = psutil.Process(12345)
p.creation_time()
>>><creation time will be different>
hash(p)
>>><hash will be different>
```

I do not know what the solution to calculating creation_time is, however, the hash could be made immutable by using the "start time in jiffies since system boot" value (value[19] from https://github.com/giampaolo/psutil/blob/5df7474cf1fcd5cb15c22c255aa55568aa5fd071/psutil/_pslinux.py#L913) directly rather than the full create_time, since that appears to be constant.

issue_title: Remove deprecated APIs
issue_body: With 3.0.0 on the way it looks like a good time to get rid of all the old cruft accumulated over the years (detailed blog post: http://grodola.blogspot.com/2014/01/psutil-20-porting.html).

issue_title: cpu_percent() disallow interval < 0
issue_body: Right now if a negative value is passed to `cpu_percent()` functions it is treated as an alias for `None` (no interval) so the functions return immediately. For consistency they should raise ValueError instead.

issue_title: Travis CI: install poppler-utils 0.41.0
issue_body:
The poppler-utils version (0.18.4) provided by Ubuntu 12.04 cannot properly render PDFs generated by rinohtype (see #2). Poppler 0.41.0 can be built on Ubuntu, so we could use that one:

```
apt-get install libopenjpeg
wget https://poppler.freedesktop.org/poppler-0.41.0.tar.xz
tar xf poppler-0.41.0.tar.xz
cd poppler-0.41.0
./configure --prefix=/usr
make
make install

```
We should package this in a .deb so it can be easily installed before running the tests on Travis.

issue_title: Confusion between versions in the documentation
issue_body:
When looking at http://mastodonpy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ the title still says "Mastodon - Mastodon 1.1.2 documentation", while actually documenting the current (developer?) version, not the 1.1.2 release.

This greatly confused me when looking up the documentation for the API for streaming endpoints, since these changed between [the 1.1.2 release](http://mastodonpy.readthedocs.io/en/1.1.2/#streaming) and the [current developer version](http://mastodonpy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/#streaming).

Secondly (Though that might be more a feature request/philosophical musing.):

When navigating to just http://mastodonpy.readthedocs.io you're automatically redirected to http://mastodonpy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ . I'm not familiar with the inner workings of readthedocs, but might an automatic redirect to http://mastodonpy.readthedocs.io/en/stable/ not make more sense? When looking up the documentation for a package I want to use, I'd expect the default docs to reflect the version available in PyPI, not the dev version, especially when the dev version changes the API.

issue_title: Proposal to treat each run through a scenario outline as a distinct scenario.
issue_body:
Currently when you iterate through a scenario outline like:

```
Scenario Outline: Winslow pages requiring user to be signed out
        Given I go to "<foo>"
       Then there should be "<bar>"

        Examples:
            | foo                  |  bar | 
            | example.com | Hi |
            | google.com   | how are you |
```

The hooks ie `@hook('before', 'scenario')` only fire at the beginning/end of the entire Scenario Outline.

In my mind it seems that each 'run' through a scenario outline should be treated as a distinct scenario.  Behavior that should be triggered before or after a scenario (as defined by the hooks) should be triggered for each outline example row.

The gherkin syntax isn't 100% clear on this https://github.com/cucumber/cucumber/wiki/Scenario-Outlines but the example at the top of the page that starts with "Copying and pasting scenarios to use different values quickly becomes tedious and repetitive:" leads some credibility to my assumptions.

Let me know if you need more details.

arguments with config file string True get automatically converted to bool
issue_body:
Supposed we have the following in the INI files:
```
value = True
```
and the add command

```
p.add( "-v","--value", type=str, default="False" ) 
```
This results in an error  ``value set to 'True' rather than a value
``(see below line 322)
But the user said it wants to have a type ``str``. So it should be parsed as a string in any case and not transformed into bool automatically, this should only happen if the user did not specify a type , or really requested a type bool. (suggestion)
(I need this to happens since I reuse the parsed stuff to interpolate  with other parsed ini files somewhere else in the code, and to make that happen everything needs to be strings )

I would use the following safe bool conversion:

```
def toBool(self,s, optionHint=None):
        """Safe converting to a boolean value, raises exception if s has wrong type or does not have the appropriate value"""
        if type(s) == str:
            try:
                if ['true','yes','on','false','no','off'].index(s.lower()) <= 2:
                    return True
                else:
                    return False
            except:
                raise ValueError("Value: %s %s !" % (s, "at option: %s" % optionsHint if optionHint else ""))
        else:
            raise ValueError("Converting type: %s with value: %s to bool is deliberately not supported!" % (type(s),s))
```

It would be good to be able to not automatically convert  to bool here (line 322):
in ``convert_setting_to_command_line_arg``
```
if value.lower() == "true":
            if type(action) not in ACTION_TYPES_THAT_DONT_NEED_A_VALUE:
                self.error("%s set to 'True' rather than a value" % key)
            args.append( action.option_strings[-1] )
``

issue_title: Writing binary data with the driver triggers a Warning
issue_body:
```
>>> a_cursor.execute('INSERT INTO binarystuff VALUES (%s)', (MySQLdb.Binary('\xdd'),))
./env/bin/ipython:1: Warning: Invalid utf8 character string: 'DD'
```

(where `binarystuff` is a table,
```sql
CREATE TABLE `binarystuff` (
  `col` varbinary(10) NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`col`)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;
```

This is because the SQL query that actually ends up being generated by the driver isn't valid. It appears to simply contain a `0xdd` byte right in the middle of the query string. (Which isn't valid UTF-8, and hence the warning. _Somehow_ MySQL does the right thing in the end, but warns you for it.)

The value needs to be escaped into a binary string literal:
```
INSERT INTO binarystuff VALUES (x'dd');
```

The  driver conflates text and binary here:

```python
def Binary(x):
    return str(x)
```

Because of this, it can't tell the difference between a `str` holding text and a `str` holding binary data, in Python 2.

Really, `Binary` (in Python 2) needs to be a real wrapper, s.t. when the time to escape the values comes, they can be properly escaped. (It seems like either hex escaping can be used, or perhaps prefixing the literal with `_binary` might work.)

In Python 3, the difference between `bytes` and `str` is more rigorous, and the type alone contains sufficient information to properly escape.

issue_title: Switching away from py-bcrypt backend
issue_body:
As passlib duly notes, all pip versions (and HEAD too) of `py-bcrypt` are vulnerable to the [BSD wraparound bug](http://www.openwall.com/lists/oss-security/2012/01/02/4):

`UserWarning: passlib.hash.bcrypt: Your installation of the 'pybcrypt' backend is vulnerable to the bsd wraparound bug, and should be upgraded or replaced with another backend (this warning will be fatal under passlib 1.7)`

Aside from being a serious security vulnerability, being fatal in passlib 1.7+ means it will also make Flask-User unusable under that version until fixed.

Alternative backends supported by passlib like [bcrypt](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/bcrypt/2.0.0) should be considered switching to.

issue_title: romanized decorator mutates ROMANIZATION_DICT
issue_body:
After `@romanized` is used, ROMANIZATION_DICT gets updated and every module importing it will get this mutated ROMANIZATION_DICT.
Snippet below should reproduce problem.
```
from mimesis import decorators, data


if __name__ == '__main__':
    print('ROMANIZATION_DICT: before')
    print(data.ROMANIZATION_DICT)

    @decorators.romanized('ru')
    def russian_name(): return 'Петр Петрович'
    # next line is where ROMANIZATION_DICT mutates
    russian_name()

    print('ROMANIZATION_DICT: after')
    print(data.ROMANIZATION_DICT)
```
Problem is here:
https://github.com/lk-geimfari/mimesis/blob/master/mimesis/decorators.py#L29

issue_title: Plumbum Idioms for Cluster Computing
issue_body:
I'm writing this system to scale the analysis of DNA sequencing and I'm building it with Plumbum, Celery and EC2.   Since the majority of DNA sequencing analysis is accomplished through pipelining many third-party executables, my idea is to build an abstract "plumbum script" and send that over the wire to AWS, where the Celery workers will accept the job and perform the actual execution.  One of plumbum's best features is being able to programmatically describe combinations of shell commands using familiar operators.  While the core of this works, I've identified a few examples where it felt like plumbum was working against me.  Please accept this use-case driven constructive feedback, and I would also appreciate any suggestions you might have.

1. Deferring execution for a remote host:

When I'm building pipelines, I'm describing commands that are not actually installed on the host (ie. client).  I realize I could utilize `Remote Commands`, but this doesn't exactly fit the design I have in mind. I think of this pattern as `Deferred Command`.  The point is, I want to execute a remote command, but I don't want Plumbum to worry about how that command is transported to the remote machine.  I just want to describe the command on one end, pickle the command, send it over the wire, unpickle it and then execute it.  Here is my solution:

```
@app.task
def plumrun(cmd):
    cmd()

def plumcmd(cmd):
    cmd = plumbum.path.LocalPath(cmd)
    return plumbum.cmd.__getattr__(cmd)

bwa = plumcmd("bwa")
samtools = plumbmd("samtools")

cmd = (bwa["mem", "-t", reference_fafn, r1_fn, r2_fn] | samtools["view", "-bS", "-"] > "/tmp/alignment.bam")
plumrun.delay(cmd)
```

The `plumcmd` is a factory method that builds `LocalPath` and `LocalCommand` classes that circumvent the need for the command to actually exist locally.  The `plumrun` is the actual Celery task that drives the remote execution.  This all works, but I am concerned that the `plumcmd` is too fragile.  I derived `plumcmd` not from the documentation, but from this [line](https://github.com/tomerfiliba/plumbum/blob/master/plumbum/machines/local.py#L200) of code within the library itself.

2. `&&` Shell Operator

Since DNA analysis typically requires a series of commands, run in serial, I've wanted to utilize the `&&` shell operator to chain commands into a single statement but it seems that Plumbum does not directly support this.  My workaround is to use Celery's Canvas `chain` operator:

```
def pipeline(*cmds):
    cmds = [plumrun.si(cmd) for cmd in cmds]
    pipeline = chain(*cmds)
    return pipeline
```

This solution works, but I was surprised to see this operator wasn't directly supported by plumbum.

3. Shell Expansion

Lots of the commands I'm executing can leverage multiple processors.  The typical idiom I utilize is to store the number of processors on a given host as a global environment variable, and utilize the shell to expand this variable.  I tried to utilize this idiom with plumbum, but it did not work:

`align_cmd = bwa["mem", "-t", "$CPU_COUNT", reference_fafn, r1_fn, r2_fn]`

`$CPU_COUNT` shoots right through to the process without expanding.  A workaround would be to explicitly run the command through a shell `sh["-c", align_cmd]`, but this feels clunky.  I know that `Popen()` allows you to set a flag to execute a command via the shell, and I tried to find a similar option within plumbum.  No dice.

In the end, Plumbum is great library, and I'm excited to add it to my toolkit.  Thanks for all the hard work.

issue_title: Planning for 1.6.0
issue_body:
I'd like to record my plans for the 1.6.0 release. Since 1.5.0 doesn't support Python 3.5, we need a release fairly soon. I can patch small fixes to make a 1.5.1 release, but I think it is probably better to do a 1.6.0, and just be ready to do a 1.6.1 if any show stoppers come up.

Here are the things I'd like to finish before we do a 1.6.0:

- [ ] Remove/simplify a little more syntax from `plumbum.colors` (like `__call__` on a style)
- [ ] Add positional argument validators (mostly done, includes a few fixes to other things) #200 
- [ ] Look at cutting back on `.COLOR*` properties for `cli.Application`, instead see if things like the program name can simply be specified with a color.
- [ ] Allow more pathlib path behavior (with a few new features, like uri's) #223 

I'm thinking on attempting a release at the end of this week

I'm hoping I'll be able to work on argument completion support for 1.7.0 (I might not have time to work on this for a while). #212 

Anyone, feel free to update or comment.

issue_title: Global awareness of Item
issue_body:
`Item` does not know anything about the `Config`(s) it is part of. That's good. But sometimes they may need to know about it. For example, when they want to know values of their siblings perhaps.

Since items are added to sections through `Config._cm__set_item`, we could make it call some hooks into `Item` being added and pass through `Config` instance to which the item is being added. That way the item is allowed to hold on to the config and use it.

Remember that by design, an `Item` cannot belong to more than one `Config` directly.

```
def _cm__set_item(self, alias, item):
    # ...
    if hasattr(item, 'item_added_hook_name'):
        getattr(item, 'item_added_hook_name')(alias=alias, config=self)
    # ...
```

Perhaps the setting of Item name that we currently do in `_cm__set_item` should be moved to the hook because we are kind of touching item's internals from outside.

issue_title: Support extra data on django storage
issue_body:
Storage adapters for ChatterBot typically support an `extra_data` attribute on statement objects which can store dynamic information about the statement. This field is currently not supported on the Django storage adapter.

I am open to suggestions on the best way to handle dynamic fields in a relational database environment.

issue_title: feature_request(path): Custom packages path support
issue_body:
### 1. Summary

It would be nice, if pip-licenses will support check licenses from custom path.

### 2. Argumentation

**Example**: I use [**Pelican — static site generator**](https://blog.getpelican.com/) and [**plugins for it**](https://github.com/getpelican/pelican-plugins), written by Python. Pelican plugins must [**installed to custom folder**](http://docs.getpelican.com/en/3.6.3/plugins.html#how-to-use-plugins) — usually [**`pelican-plugins`**](https://github.com/Kristinita/KristinitaPelican/tree/master/pelican-plugins), not to default `site-packages`. I don't know, how possible check licenses from `pelican-plugins` folder.

### 3. Example of expected behavior

**If**:

    pip-licenses --path output/pelican-plugins

pip-licenses will check licenses from `output/pelican-plugins` folder recursively.

### 4. Not helped

I don't find already existing packages, that solve this problem. For example, I try:

+ [**license_lister**](https://github.com/jonkr/license_lister),
+ [**license_info**](https://pypi.org/project/license-info/),
+ [**plicense**](https://pypi.org/project/plicense/),
+ [**yolk3k**](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/yolk3k),
+ [**pylicense**](https://pypi.org/project/pylicense/).

Thanks.

issue_body:
Hi there,

I'm trying to package up my python app that uses pywinusb with pyinstaller. 

I wrote a little Test.py:

```
import pywinusb.hid as hid

all_devices = hid.find_all_hid_devices()

if not all_devices:
    print("No devices found")

for device in all_devices:
    print(device)
```
This works fine and I get a print out of a list of devices like:
```
HID device (vID=0x04f3, pID=0x20d0, v=0x1111); ELAN; Touchscreen, Path: \\?\hid#vid_04f3&pid_20d0&col04#6&11e83ff1&0&0003#{4d1e55b2-f16f-11cf-88cb-001111000030}

When I then try to package it up using pyinstaller, it prints out `No devices found`.

issue_title: Fix waits/locks to cope with uninterruptible locking
issue_body:
I've recently discovered that some versions of Python (at least 2.7 - seems fixed in 3.6) are entirely uninterruptible when waiting on locks with no "timeout" parameter, i.e. it'll retry on every EINTR from sem_wait with no way to customize that behavior.

This could mean shutting down an executor can hang indefinitely and may not be interruptible by SIGTERM/SIGINT.  To minimize problems relating to this, I think any possible calls to a wait/join/etc from the main thread should be reviewed and possibly given a timeout parameter, which makes it interruptible.

issue_title: Movie loudness compression
issue_body:
I've used this for an old series which has really bad sound mixing (starts with Doctor) on the remastered BluRay/DVD releases:

`ffmpeg-normalize --verbose --merge --force --acodec libfdk_aac --ebu --extra-options "-threads 4 -b:a 224k -filter:a 'compand=0|0:1|1:-90/-900|-70/-70|-30/-9|0/-3:6:0:0:0'" *.mkv`

Perhaps this helps someone who doesn't only want to normalize, but to even out peaks and lows, also.

issue_title: Add EnumField
issue_body:
MySQL's ENUM type has no Django field apart from [on Stack Overflow](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/21454/specifying-a-mysql-enum-in-a-django-model). This is a shame for people using legacy DBs.

Similar to #136 and #91, we should add an EnumField that allows using these columns. A bonus would be to get migrations going smoothly such that adding/removing a single enum value would go well - the `ALTER TABLE` should be instant.

Create audit_table trigger in alembic migration
issue_body:
Why isn't the `audit_table()` trigger added to new tables that are created with `alembic revision --autogenerate`? Am I correct in suspecting that Alembic doesn't support this?

If so, perhaps we could add a section in the documentation about using [alembic's rewriter functionality](http://alembic.readthedocs.org/en/latest/api/autogenerate.html#alembic.autogenerate.rewriter.Rewriter) to achieve this instead. This is how I'm currently solving it in my `env.py`:

```python
from alembic import context
from alembic.autogenerate import rewriter
from alembic.operations import ops

writer = rewriter.Rewriter()


@writer.rewrites(ops.CreateTableOp)
def create_table(context, revision, op):
    sql = "sa.select([sa.func.audit_table('%s')])" % op.table_name
    return [op, ops.ExecuteSQLOp(sql)]


def run_migrations_online():
    context.configure(
        process_revision_directives=writer,
...
```

It should be noted that the `__versioned__` attribute isn't respected when doing it this way. AFAIK the model isn't available in this context, but perhaps `__versioned__` could be passed to the table. Maybe you have some idea on how to achieve this, @kvesteri?

issue_title: postgresql bin path discovery
issue_body:
Hi, im submitting a PR to add RHEL/CentOS path but opening an issue because I think it would be good to reconsider how this implemented.

Right now its searching a bunch of paths for the postgresql binaries, but as you are already seeing hard coding these paths is not a good idea because it can vary.

On top of the bin dir being in different places, if you take the approach of "lets search for the first bin dir we can find", then we as testers have no control over which postgresql version gets used.

Your current implementation kind of makes the assumption that theres only one postgresql version per system which is not always true. For example, im working in CentOS 7 and if I install postgresql from the standard RHEL repo its at 9.2. However, I need to use 9.5 for testing because it has newer features I need, so I add the postgresql yum repo and install 9.5 (this can be done along side 9.2 without having to uninstall it). Now if I have both installed, how do I tell testing.postgresql which to choose?

I have submitted the patch in the current style but I would recommend it be changed in the future to use os.environ['PATH'] instead so we can just put the path to the postgresql bin dir that we want into the beginning  of our PATH and you dont have to search for the directory, you just always execute the version found first in PATH.

I havent read through all the code but if its not only the bin dir we need then maybe have it look for PG_HOME env var or something if you also need the other postgresql folders relative to this.

issue_title: Feature request: independent order counters for each specific foreign key value
issue_body:
Hello and thanks for your useful work!

I've plugged your Django battery for my custom model, say, `Model_1` that has a foreign key to other model, say, `Model_2`.

`Order` field automatically increments on creating new values in `Model_1`, but it's the same incrementation throughout the whole model.

| pk | title    | order  | model_2_fk |
|----|---------|---------|-----------------|
| 1  | Title 1 | 1        | fk_one         |
| 2  | Title 2 | 2        | fk_one         |
| 3  | Title 3 | 3        | fk_two         |
| 4  | Title 4 | 4        | fk_two         |
| 5  | Title 5 | 5        | fk_three      |
| 6  | Title 6 | 6        | fk_three      |

It would be great to start ordering from 1 for each foreign key value linked with the model, to make separate ordering for each so-called 'category' coming from the FK value.

| pk | title    | order  | model_2_fk |
|----|---------|---------|-----------------|
| 1  | Title 1 | 1        | fk_one         |
| 2  | Title 2 | 2        | fk_one         |
| 3  | Title 3 | 1        | fk_two         |
| 4  | Title 4 | 2        | fk_two         |
| 5  | Title 5 | 1        | fk_three      |
| 6  | Title 6 | 2        | fk_three      |

So, basically, when admin adds a new value in Model_1 the plugin should get the max value of ordering field for the current FK value from another model and increment ordering field for each FK values independently.

There should be such options for admin.py:
- [ ] a `BooleanField` to turn this behaviour on or not, probably being `False` by default.
- [ ] an option to specify which FK field of the current model should be taken into account in such context.

I hope I've explained my request clear enough. I'd be glad for some implementation in the future. ;-)

def render_options(self, selected_choices):

def render_options(self, choices, selected_choices):

issue_title: Performance issue when populate millions documents
issue_body:
##### Problem
When we need to put a lot of documents in index, we need to use `queryset_pagination` meta option to paginate. Django pagination need a sorted queryset with `order_by` (cf doc)[https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/2.0/topics/pagination/#required-arguments] otherwise same pk can be present more than once.

Put `order_by` on queryset will make django paginator call order_by for each page. Call `order_by` on huge queryset (like 10 millions) will lead to a huge perfomance issue.

##### Temporary solution:

We can override `_get_actions` method (from `django_elasticsearch_dsl.documents.DocType`) to not use django paginator when a queryset is passed. More over because of the way a database index work, we should first fetch only pks, and then do sub request based on it.

```python
from django.db.models.query import QuerySet


def _get_actions(self, object_list, action):
    if self._doc_type.queryset_pagination and isinstance(object_list, QuerySet):
        pks = object_list.order_by('pk').values_list('pk', flat=True)
        for start_pk_index in range(0, len(pks), self._doc_type.queryset_pagination + 1):
            end_pk_index = start_pk_index + self._doc_type.queryset_pagination
            ranged_qs = object_list.filter(pk__range=[
                pks[start_pk_index],
                pks[end_pk_index]
            ])
            for object_instance in ranged_qs:
                yield self._prepare_action(object_instance, action)
    else:
        super()._get_actions(object_list, action)
```

Available to make the PR if needed.

issue_title: ad video failed to upload with windows path provided or utf-8 chars in path
issue_body:
code 
```python
video = AdVideo(parent_id='act_xxxxxxxxx')
video[AdVideo.Field.filepath] = r'C:\some\path\to\video.mp4'
video.remote_create()
```
execute failed with error `Video Upload Time Out`:
```json
{"error":{"message":"Service temporarily unavailable","type":"OAuthException","is_transient":true,"code":2,"error_subcode":1363030,"error_user_title":"Video Upload Time Out","error_user_msg":"Your video upload timed out before it could be completed. This is probably because of a slow network connection or because the video you're trying to upload is too large. Please try again.","fbtrace_id":"Fbxxxxxx"}}
```
but
```python
video[AdVideo.Field.filepath] = 'video.mp4' # in relative path
video[AdVideo.Field.filepath] = '/some/path/to/video.mp4' # on linux or macOS
```
works fine.

This is caused by the requests lib that change the `filename` header in `Content-Disposition` into `filename*=utf-8` header when the `\` path provided.  While the facebook graph-video server seems do not recognize the `filename*=utf-8` header

Problem of windows path without utf-8 char in filename can be solved by change 
https://github.com/facebook/facebook-python-business-sdk/blob/e2f279a62b7e6c7fd30a080b4be8103c7303a715/facebook_business/video_uploader.py#L216
into
```python
os.path.basename(context.file_path)
```

issue_title: Travis QA job fails on isort, sometimes
issue_body:
I have merged two pull request by @jaroel, which themselves passed fine on Travis, but now on master Travis fails. This is only the [QA job](https://travis-ci.org/plone/plone.api/jobs/279411379).

- `isort` complains. When I try it locally it complains too, about `portal.py` which has the date/datetime commits in the wrong sort order. When I fix it locally, it complains again about those same lines. When I revert the change, it passes again. That is really weird. Same for the `flake8` call.

- In the `docs` jobs we get a too new `Products.ZCatalog` which cannot do `from AccessControl.Permission import getPermissionIdentifier`. AccessControl indeed does not have that. Oh, I see that this part [failed](https://travis-ci.org/plone/plone.api/jobs/279155753) on the original pull requests as well. And the `docs` part of Travis actually passes on master. It is just the isort/flake8 that fails.

Maybe we should just retry later.

issue_title: Difference between behavior of jsonls.js on Playground and pyld
RiakBucket creation *still* not free of race conditions [JIRA: CLIENTS-439]
issue_body:
We see None being returned from RiakClient.bucket(..) a few dozen times per day on our worker processes. This is probably exacerbated by the fact we're forcing garbage collection in another thread periodically.

This has been tackled in https://github.com/basho/riak-python-client/commit/f944a8b6174bf17e2c57caa386bd199944a1f6c8 by using WeakValueDictionary.setdefault, but until http://bugs.python.org/issue19542 is merged in (status still open), setdefault isn't atomic itself, hence a race condition returning None is possible.

issue_title: GooglePlus decoder needed
issue_body:
I believe the default decoder provided by RAuth.utils is incompatible with GooglePlus via OAuth2 decoding schema.  Default RAuth decoder when used by GooglePlus, results in an empty dict {}.

I used this following decoder which seems to work just fine as I passed this into RAuth's get_auth_session() and got the desired tokens.


    @staticmethod
    def decoder(data: bytes, encoding: str='utf-8') -> dict:
        """
        Provide a decoder for RAuth to invoke to unpack the response and decode accordingly

        :param data: bytes
            Byte data stream that needs to be decoded (handled by this decoder)
        :param encoding: str
            Encoding for the bytes

        :return:
            Dictionary with the values returned by Google Plus

        :rtype: dict
        """
        result = data.decode(encoding)
        if result:
            json = loads(result)
            return json
        else:
            return {}

issue_title: Can TraitType init_instance depend on object initialization arguments?
issue_body:
I am trying to replace my traitlet-like system with the traitlets project.  I have a number of "`Cluster`" objects that have a size member, which is provided to the constructor.  They contain numpy array traits that should be sized according to this `size`:

````python
    class ClusterSizedNDArray(NDArray):

        def __init__(self, **metadata):
            super().__init__(cluster_sized=True,
                             shape=(),
                             dtype=np.float64,
                             **metadata)

        def shape(self, obj):
            return (obj.size,) + self.metadata['shape']


    class Cluster(HasTraits):
        
        signals = ClusterSizedNDArray()

        def __new__(cls, *args, **kw):
            new_meth = super().__new__
            size = kw.pop('size')
            obj = new_meth(cls, **kw)
            # Initialize size before traits are populated that have that size.
            obj.size = size
            return obj
        
````

Unfortunately, this doesn't work because `obj.size` is still not set on the object until after `HasTraits.__new__` has been called.  What would be a nice way to make this work?  Is this something that `traitlets` can handle or should I implement my own similar system?

Perhaps I should inherit from another class that just sets the size, so that the mro is `Cluster, HasTraits, SizeSettingBaseClass`?

FileSystemFilter should forward all interface functions to wrapped IOSystem instance
issue_body:
I'm trying to override the [`IOSystem::PushDirectory`](https://github.com/assimp/assimp/blob/v4.1.0/include/assimp/IOSystem.hpp#L190), [`IOSystem::CurrentDirectory`](https://github.com/assimp/assimp/blob/v4.1.0/include/assimp/IOSystem.hpp#L197), [`IOSystem::StackSize`](https://github.com/assimp/assimp/blob/v4.1.0/include/assimp/IOSystem.hpp#L203) and [`IOSystem::PopDirectory`](https://github.com/assimp/assimp/blob/v4.1.0/include/assimp/IOSystem.hpp#L210) functions. Sadly my overloads are never called.

The problem seems to be, that the [`BaseImporter::ReadFile`](https://github.com/assimp/assimp/blob/v4.1.0/code/BaseImporter.cpp#L80) function [wraps](https://github.com/assimp/assimp/blob/v4.1.0/code/BaseImporter.cpp#L89) my `IOSystem` instance inside a `FileSystemFilter` and the `FileSystemFilter` doesn't forward the above mentioned functions to my `IOSystem` instance. Therefore just the default implementations will be called.

Other virtual `IOSystem` functions ([`IOSystem::CreateDirectory`](https://github.com/assimp/assimp/blob/v4.1.0/include/assimp/IOSystem.hpp#L218), [`IOSystem::ChangeDirectory`](https://github.com/assimp/assimp/blob/v4.1.0/include/assimp/IOSystem.hpp#L225) and [`IOSystem::DeleteFile`](https://github.com/assimp/assimp/blob/v4.1.0/include/assimp/IOSystem.hpp#L227)) probably also can't be overridden.

issue_title: glTF 2.0 meshes without indices result in empty meshes
issue_body:
The [TriangleWithoutIndices.gltf](https://github.com/KhronosGroup/glTF-Sample-Models/blob/master/2.0/TriangleWithoutIndices/glTF-Embedded/TriangleWithoutIndices.gltf) model demonstrates this issue. To reproduce grab the file and run it through the assimp export and observe that no mesh geometry is created in the output.

Meshes which do not have indices result in a glTF::Mesh::Primitive with a null indicies property (as it should be).

When glTFImporter::ImportMeshes processes the meshes it has a check for prim.indices and only if it's defined does it create the triangle data. The glTF 2.0 specification states the indices property is optional for primitives and when not present the same semantics as WebGL drawArrays() should be used to construct the mesh. Currently if prim.indices is not defined nothing gets made and mNumFaces end up as 0 and you have an empty mesh but with all of the vertex, normal etc data defined.

Willing to have a go at addressing this but wanted to log an issue first and see if others had already looked at it or if my interpretation may be wrong? Looks like a fall through case is needed that makes the mesh in the same way that drawArrays would when prim.indices is not defined, but I'll need to investigate further.

My guess is someone already looked at this and it's not as straight forward as I am assuming so any comments would be appreciated.

I didn't think I would encounter much non-indexed glTF mesh data in the wild but I was just handed two files with exactly that issue and half the geometry is missing as a result so seems it's an important case to handle.

issue_title: Beat scheduler crashes due to race condition
issue_body:
### Problem

Previously reported by @poloping on the celery project tracker, and encountered by myself as well - if a schedule gets updated just around the time that the 3 minute sync cycle elapses, one of the sync operations gets a closed connection and the scheduler crashes.

I've traced it to a previous fix, in this pull request https://github.com/celery/django-celery-beat/pull/58, where a call to `close_old_connections()` was added to the sync method. This PR seems to fix an issue for MySQL but breaks **postgresql** in my case. I'm using **CONN_MAX_AGE = 0**

celery 4.2.0
django-celery-beat 1.1.1

issue_title: lambda function invoked more than once with client.invoke
issue_body:
I have a lambda function whose responsible of invoking another lambda function.   

# **use case** 
Listen to s3 events and perform multiple functions on the event. 
As currently an event can only trigger **one lambda function** I coded  a 'controller' function that listens to an s3 event and forwards the event to the other functions using the client.invoke method.    
I have an exactly-once policy on those functions, so I am logging the s3 event in multiple places in the code, to see how many times each function was executed per event.   

The setup is  this:  
s3 event --> controller --> multiple functions (lets say A and B). 
I'm using `innocationType="Event"`
code:   
```
log.info(event[])    
for function in functionNames:
     try:
        client.invoke(FunctionName=function,InvocationType=innocationType,Qualifier=qaulifier,Payload=json.dumps(event))
       log.info(event + function)
    except:
```

This is at least where I started with, I added more checks to the invoke method (See Later)

# **Tests performed**

1. 

First thing that I checked is to see if I have a problem with A or B.    
For each function separately I connected the s3 trigger to it and saw that each event is only executed once. So there is not a problem here.   

2.

I am logging hoe many times:
The controller was called
The controller tried to invoke the function (I have a retry on that )
Function A was called
Function B was called

The results are (an example of 100 Put events events):
The controller was called - 100 times
The controller tried to invoke the function (I have a retry on that )  - 100 for each function
Function A was called - 105 times
Function B was called - 108 times

This shows me that the only thing that could go wrong is that for some reason `client.invoke` called the function more than 1 time for some events.

# **Measures I tried**
1.

I tried to put a timeout on the `invoke` call using a decorator:
```
@timeout_decorator.timeout(0.800)
def invoke(client,function,qaulifier,event,innocationType="Event"):
    r = client.invoke(FunctionName=function,InvocationType=innocationType,Qualifier=qaulifier,Payload=json.dumps(event))
    return r
```

2.

according to : [https://github.com/boto/boto3/issues/1104#issuecomment-305136266](url)
I tried to set a limit `client.meta.events._unique_id_handlers['retry-config-lambda']['handler']._checker.__dict__['_max_attempts'] = 0`. no luck


# **What's next**
1. Is this a known issue ?
2. How can I debug it ?
3. Alternative solution to my use case ?

issue_body:
I have been using the API successfully to integrate Categories and Products with existing legacy systems a couple of weeks ago. Developed everything and now it is time to test in the production environment. While in Dvmt environment each time I executed 

r = wcapi.get("products/categories?per_page=100")

the Json generated was like (reduced attributed for simplicity)
[

  { "id":790,

Now without changing anything except installing WP-REST-API plugin on WordPress after executing

r = wcapi.get("products/categories?per_page=100")

issue_title: Diff fails on empty repository.
issue_body:
If you have an empty repository (`git init` in an empty folder), then add files through GitPython, the repository isn't registered as "dirty" and diffs come back empty. For example:

    >>> r = Repo('.')
    >>> r.index.add(["README"])
    >>> r.is_dirty
    False
    >>> r.index.diff(None)
    []

The index.entries property shows the newly added file, but there doesn't seem to be a way to turn it into "this file has been added, but not committed".

issue_title: Problem Pipfile and system specific packages
issue_body:
Specifying certain packages to be installed only for some operating systems sometimes works, but sometimes not. For me it is difficult to overcome the problem of pyqt5 and pypiwin32 package entries in Pipfile when deploying on Linux. 

##### Describe your environment

1. OS: `Ubuntu 17.04`
2. Python version: `3.5.3`
3. Pipenv version: `11.0.2`

##### Example
In my Pipfile the `[packages]` entry looks like this (as per the doc example):

```
[packages]
"pypiwin32" = {version = "*", os_name = "== 'windows'"}
```
or like this:
```
[packages]
"pyqt5" = {version = "*", os_name = "== 'windows'"}
```

##### Expected result

In the `pypiwin32` example I simply expected this package not to be installed on other platforms than Windows. Self-explanatory. 

In the `pyqt5` example I would actually like to install this packages on all systems but Ubuntu, since for whatever reason on Ubuntu we can't install pyqt5 with pip anyways. However, since I was unable to specify this package to be emitted on Ubuntu only, therefore I want to it to be excluded on all Linux distributions.

Or at least let the warning be thrown but I would like the rest of the packages listed in Pipfile to be installed at least.

##### Actual result

In `pypiwin32` example:
```
Warning: Your dependencies could not be resolved. You likely have a mismatch in your sub-dependencies.
  You can use $ pipenv install --skip-lock to bypass this mechanism, then run $ pipenv graph to inspect the situation.
Could not find a version that matches pywin32>=223
Tried: (no version found at all)
```
In `pyqt5` example:
```
Warning: Your dependencies could not be resolved. You likely have a mismatch in your sub-dependencies.
  You can use $ pipenv install --skip-lock to bypass this mechanism, then run $ pipenv graph to inspect the situation.
Could not find a version that matches pyqt5
Tried: (no version found at all)
```

As you can see it did not matter if I specified to not install those packages - the error still occurs. In fact it does not matter at all what I write after the `"packagename" =` bit because apparently the packages themselves just being there listed cause some problems. And non of the 'correct' packages that might be listed in Pipfile will be installed

And just for the record, the docs example of doing exactly the same thing (with `pywinusb = {version = "*", os_name = "== 'windows'"}`) worked seamlessly.

##### Steps to replicate
Pipfile
```
[[source]]

url = "https://pypi.python.org/simple"
verify_ssl = true
name = "pypi"


[packages]

"pypiwin32" = {version = "*", os_name = "== 'windows'"}
"pyqt5" = {version = "*", os_name = "== 'windows'"}
requests = "*"


[dev-packages]


[requires]

```
- `pipenv --python 3.5`
- `pipenv install` or `pipenv update`

issue_title: Hang when attempting to discover offset manager
issue_body:
PyKafka v2.1.2
Python 3.5

I have noticed that occasionally I will experience one of my tests hanging, specifically when the offset manager is trying to be discovered with a BalancedConsumer.

The topic is being created dynamically from my test case.

```
2016-01-18 22:11:02,093| shepherd.orgstreamer/77: Getting new Kafka consumer... @_get_kafka_consumer:112 #DEBUG
2016-01-18 22:11:02,097| pykafka.handlers/77: RequestHandler.stop: about to flush requests queue @stop:134 #INFO
2016-01-18 22:11:02,097| pykafka.topic/77: Adding 1 partitions @update:147 #INFO
2016-01-18 22:11:02,098| kazoo.client/77: Connecting to kafka:2181 @_connect:575 #INFO
2016-01-18 22:11:02,102| kazoo.client/77: Zookeeper connection established, state: CONNECTED @_session_callback:444 #INFO
2016-01-18 22:11:02,134| pykafka.balancedconsumer/77: Rebalancing consumer "74f0aa9b3750:bed7ddf6-d74b-4864-bd00-daf837cebece" for topic "b'vk.datarouter.data.filtered.shepherd.test.2'". @_rebalance:518 #INFO
2016-01-18 22:11:02,136| pykafka.balancedconsumer/77: Balancing 1 participants for 1 partitions.
Owning 1 partitions. @_decide_partitions:403 #INFO
2016-01-18 22:11:02,140| pykafka.cluster/77: Attempting to discover offset manager for consumer group 'b'shepherd.test.2'' @get_offset_manager:346 #INFO
```

And then it just hangs indefinitely. I have not yet tracked down the cause of this, but will investigate further and try to get a reproducible test case.


Update 01/20/2016: 
This is an annoying bug as it's preventing my automated tests from running reliably. It _seems_ to be only happening when attempting to consume (or maybe just creating the BalancedConsumer?) the first time with a new Kafka cluster.
It looks to be hanging at BalancedConsumer._rebalance() while it is waiting for the _rebalancing_lock.

issue_title: pyltp 0.1.9.1 conflict with anaconda 4.2.0
issue_body:
I intall pyltp by pip in anaconda 4.2.0  Ubuntu16.04 LTS envirnment, I get a problem when I run `import pyltp`:
```
... undefined symbol: _ZTVNSt7__cxx1119basic_istringstreamIcSt11char_traitsIcESaIcEEE
```
and then I install by source, still have the same problem.  I try to use `sudo python setup.py intall` and add package path in sys.path like:
```
import sys
sys.path.append(/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pyltp-0.1.9.1-py2.7-linux-x86_64.egg)
import pyltp
```
error message become:

```
 /home/?/anaconda2/bin/../lib/libstdc++.so.6: version `GLIBCXX_3.4.20' not found (required by /usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pyltp-0.1.9.1-py2.7-linux-x86_64.egg/pyltp.so)
```
So I think the problem come from anaconda 4.2.0 envirnment libstdc++,  I run ` strings /home/ldy/anaconda2/bin/../lib/libstdc++.so.6 | grep GLIBCXX`

```
GLIBCXX_3.4
GLIBCXX_3.4.1
GLIBCXX_3.4.2
GLIBCXX_3.4.3
GLIBCXX_3.4.4
GLIBCXX_3.4.5
GLIBCXX_3.4.6
GLIBCXX_3.4.7
GLIBCXX_3.4.8
GLIBCXX_3.4.9
GLIBCXX_3.4.10
GLIBCXX_3.4.11
GLIBCXX_3.4.12
GLIBCXX_3.4.13
GLIBCXX_3.4.14
GLIBCXX_3.4.15
GLIBCXX_3.4.16
GLIBCXX_3.4.17
GLIBCXX_3.4.18
GLIBCXX_3.4.19
GLIBCXX_FORCE_NEW
GLIBCXX_DEBUG_MESSAGE_LENGTH
```
There is no `GLIBCXX_3.4.20`  in anaconda 4.2.0 libstdc++

**Solution**:
my solution is sample:
```
cd ~/anaconda2/lib
rm libstdc++.so.6.0.19
ln -s /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libstdc++.so.6 libstdc++.so.6.0.19
```

check `strings libstdc++.so.6 | grep GLIBCXX`, you will find the following info:
```
...
GLIBCXX_3.4.16
GLIBCXX_3.4.17
GLIBCXX_3.4.18
GLIBCXX_3.4.19
GLIBCXX_3.4.20
GLIBCXX_3.4.21
GLIBCXX_3.4.22
GLIBCXX_DEBUG_MESSAGE_LENGTH
```

I write down this to help other people who meet the same problem.

issue_title: BibUpload: optional use of bibxxx tables
issue_body:
Originally on 2011-06-14

During bibupload, the incoming record is broken according to MARC tags
into many bibxxx tables (`bib10x`, `bib11x`, etc) which results in
many SQL queries being done by bibupload.  Advantage is doing so is
that the end users can then simply search in any MARC
tag. Disadvantage in doing so is that the uploading step takes time,
and that we are preparing indexes that may perhaps not even be used by
the end users at all.  (Since they typically search in logical field
indexes, say `firstauthor:ellis`, not in physical MARC tags, say
`100__a:/ellis/`.)

In certain situations, it would be better not to create these indexes
during upload time, but to defer handing them for the indexing time.
(Especially when using external indexer such as Solr for the record
the metadata.)

For this, it would be good to introduce a new configuration option
called say `CFG_BIBUPLOAD_USE_BIBXXX` that would be True by default
but that could optionally be set to False on a per-site basis.  When
set to False, the stage 4 of bibupload (=filling of bibxxx tables)
would not be executed.

This would result in bibupload speed-ups that can be illustrated by
the following example taken from INSPIRE-sized database (1M of
records):

- example record CERN-TH-6002-91 from INSPIRE TEST (record ID 315385)

- timings to replace it, stage 4 enabled:

```
   ncalls  tottime  percall  cumtime  percall filename:lineno(function)
        1    0.006    0.006    4.112    4.112 bibupload.py:162(bibupload)
      256    0.003    0.000    4.095    0.016 dbquery.py:141(run_sql)
        1    0.001    0.001    2.632    2.632 bibupload.py:1550(update_database_with_metadata)
      109    0.001    0.000    2.605    0.024 bibupload.py:822(insert_record_bibxxx)
        1    0.000    0.000    1.255    1.255 bibupload.py:1780(delete_bibrec_bibxxx)
```

- timings to replace it, stage 4 disabled:

```
   ncalls  tottime  percall  cumtime  percall filename:lineno(function)
        1    0.000    0.000    0.020    0.020 bibupload.py:162(bibupload)
       37    0.001    0.000    0.017    0.000 dbquery.py:141(run_sql)
        1    0.000    0.000    0.006    0.006 bibupload.py:1780(delete_bibrec_bibxxx)
```

As can be seen, the upload time is faster by several orders of
magnitude, since we are not pre-creating those huge and possibly
non-useful bibxxx indexes.

-*Important note:** while it is simple to introduce such a
`CFG_BIBUPLOAD_USE_BIBXXX` variable for record uploading processes,
this variable should be propagated to other Invenio modules such as
searcher/indexer that should read record metadata from pre-stored
MARCXML formats (see table `bibfmt`) rather than from `bibxxx` tables.
When `bibxxx` tables are not in use, other Invenio modules are not
free to rely on the existence of `bibxxx` tables anymore.  So this
task is really bigger than it may seem.  The settings of
`CFG_BIBUPLOAD_USE_BIBXXX` should therefore be progressively
propagated to all the Invenio modules that assume the existence of
`bibxxx` for granted, starting with the the most important modules
(indexer, searcher, editor, check for deleted records, etc).

issue_title: knowledge: rest api issues
issue_body:
@hachreak I had look at the new knowledge REST API looks great - just have some few minor comments that I'd like to fix:

- [x] ``/api/knowledges`` -> ``/api/knowledge`` (knowledge is uncountable, thus no plural)
- [x] ``/api/knowledges/<int:id>`` -> ``/api/knowledge/<str:id>`` i think it would make more sense for an end-user to access ``/api/knowledges/licenses/`` than ``/api/knowledges/2`` and ``name`` is already unique in the database model so it should be fine.
- [x]  Possibly allow disabling REST API access to a mapping (to ensure that not everything is exposed).

issue_title: pylibpd failes to compile
issue_body:
Probably due to the old recipe (2013) the distribute script fails to compile pylibpd.
I've noticed that in the pylibpd recipe setup.py build and install are executed before
compiling the libpd itself.
In addition to this the distribute script cannot download any of the libpd submodules.
This because the script download the .zip version of the repo and github does not include
submodules using this kind of download.
Using git to clone the repository would fix the problem, by adding the
"git submodule init" and "git submodule update" commands.

issue_title: Base: Failed to import "android" module. Could not remove android presplash.
issue_body:
This occurs after removing the .buildozer folder, and using the latest master checkouts of buildozer and python-for-android. This occurs when trying to use the 'android_old' target (the 'android' gives us this error: https://github.com/kivy/python-for-android/issues/1142)

python-for-android: commit 2136e0f26e2516c98159291b2edf60b84569809d
buildozer: commit 3a93783aa8cbfb0605e4b46f977f8c9951c1a043

This means our python service does not start and our background downloads to not start.

This happens even if we add android to the requirements. From the buildozer.spec file.

    requirements = python, sqlite3, kivy==1.10.0, openssl, pyjnius, android

I can see where this error is throw (kivy.base) but the only files containing that text in the .buildozer folder are pyc and pyo files. 

    .buildozer/android/platform/python-for-android/dist/xxx/python-install/lib/python2.7/site-packages/kivybase.py

Any ideas' on how to solve this? Or where one makes changes to alter this?

issue_title: Verb/route combinations appear in swagger documentation output that are not in the code
issue_body:
Permutations of routes and verbs appear as documented operations in the swagger output that aren't present in the code.  Other applications rely on the JSON output of swagger, and the superfluity 

E.g. The code defines a route at "/edges/" with "GET" and "POST" methods.  The code also defines a route at "/edges/<int:annotation_id>/" with "GET", "PUT", "PATCH", and "DELETE" methods.  [This is a total of 2 routes and 5 method verbs.]  The swagger output shows a list of 10 operations, which includes combinations of verbs and routes that don't exist in code (e.g. "POST" for the "/edges/<int:annotation_id>/" route).

issue_title: plot_mean_quantile_returns_spread_time_series seems failed with dataframe input
issue_body:
# Problem
When I substitute my local data in `tear_sheet_walk_through.ipynb` and call `alphalens.plotting.plot_mean_quantile_returns_spread_time_series(quant_return_spread, std_err_spread)`, 

I got :
>Traceback (most recent call last):
>  File "D:\Anaconda3\lib\site-packages\alphalens\plotting.py", line 494, in plot_mean_quantile_returns_spread_time_series
>    ax=a)
> AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'get_ylim'

`qutant_return_spread` and `std_err_spread` is calculated with my local data, which look like:

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/17619612/21482452/2a9a528a-cbaf-11e6-8aca-874306fb3fd6.png)
![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/17619612/21482454/3539a8e4-cbaf-11e6-8d96-f50b9e62bd48.png)

# Some thoughts
I think the problem might be caused by the first if block in the definition of `plot_mean_quantile_returns_spread_time_series`. 
```python

    if isinstance(mean_returns_spread, pd.DataFrame):
        if ax is None:
            ax = [None for a in mean_returns_spread.columns]

        ymin, ymax = (None, None)
        for a, (name, fr_column) in zip(ax, mean_returns_spread.iteritems()):
            stdn = None if std_err is None else std_err[name]
            a = plot_mean_quantile_returns_spread_time_series(fr_column,
                                                                   std_err=stdn,
                                                                   ax=a)
            curr_ymin, curr_ymax = a.get_ylim()
            ymin = curr_ymin if ymin is None else min(ymin, curr_ymin)
            ymax = curr_ymax if ymax is None else max(ymax, curr_ymax)                                                          

        for a in ax:
            a.set_ylim([ymin, ymax])

        return ax
```


When passed a dataframe and `ax` is `None`, the code sets `ax` to be a list of `None`s. In my example, it is `[None, None, None]`. And then it calls itself on each column of the dataframe, with key argument `ax=a`. 
```python
a = plot_mean_quantile_returns_spread_time_series(fr_column,
                                                                                   std_err=stdn,
                                                                                   ax=a)
```


Here, `a` is still `None`, so when it comes to `curr_ymin, curr_ymax = a.get_ylim()`, it raises an error 
> AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'get_ylim'



Consequently, the first column is successfully plotted, but the subsequent columns failed.


To fix that, I reset `a` to be the output of the recurrsive call, and the replace the corresponding element list `ax` with it, so that `a.get_ylim()` have the right metho :
```python
        for (i, a), (name, fr_column) in zip(enumerate(ax), mean_returns_spread.iteritems()):
            stdn = None if std_err is None else std_err[name]
            a = plot_mean_quantile_returns_spread_time_series(fr_column,
                                                                   std_err=stdn,
                                                                   ax=a)
            ax[i] = a
            curr_ymin, curr_ymax = a.get_ylim()
```

I am new to this problem. This is the only solution to my problem. Any better way?

issue_title: Allow HSM adaptive moments to use circular weight function
issue_body: The HSM adaptive moments code that is exposed uses an elliptical weight function when computing moments.  There is also existing code in the HSM source file to use a circular weight function.  This issue is to modify the call to the adaptive moments code to allow for the use of a circular weight function.

issue_title: Global configuration file throws warning when group/other read permissions
issue_body:
### Summary
When setting a configuration file for tower-cli with a globe scope, the file generated is only read and writable by the original user. Setting read permissions to the global file then produces warnings at run-time which cannot be turned off.

### Environment

* Tower version: 3.1.1
*Tower-cli version: 3.1.3

### Steps To Reproduce:

1. Create config with a global scope (as root or with sudo):

```
tower-cli config username user --scope=global
tower-cli config password secret --scope=global
```

2. Review permissions for /etc/tower/tower_cli.cfg
3. Switch to a different (or under privileged) user
4. Run `tower-cli config` or test credentials via `tower-cli inventory list` and verify that the global credentials are not accessible
5. Switch back to the original or privileged user and modify /etc/tower/tower_cli.cfg to be readable by group and/or other
6. Jump back to the under privileged user and run `tower-cli inventory list`

### Expected Results:

```
== ============== ============ 
id      name      organization 
== ============== ============ 
 1 Demo Inventory            1
== ============== ============ 
```

### Actual Results:

```
/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/tower_cli/conf.py:47: RuntimeWarning: File /etc/tower/tower_cli.cfg readable by group or others.
  .format(fpname), RuntimeWarning)
== ============== ============ 
id      name      organization 
== ============== ============ 
 1 Demo Inventory            1
== ============== ============
```

### Additional Information:

Workaround to remove warning:

1. Navigate to /usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/tower_cli/
2. Modify conf.py with the following:

Change (lines 44-47):
```
            if (file_permission.st_mode & stat.S_IRGRP) or \
               (file_permission.st_mode & stat.S_IROTH):
                warnings.warn('File {0} readable by group or others.'
                              .format(fpname), RuntimeWarning)
```

To (lines 44-47):
```
#            if (file_permission.st_mode & stat.S_IRGRP) or \
#               (file_permission.st_mode & stat.S_IROTH):
#                warnings.warn('File {0} readable by group or others.'
#                              .format(fpname), RuntimeWarning)
```

issue_title: Week 1 Sprint
automatically order by when styling by a column
issue_body:
Mapnik draws based on the order in the table, so if styling by values, the highest ones should be drawn on top. This should happen behind the scenes, but a `kwarg` for ordering should be available for the user to overwrite the default behavior.

For example, if a user enters this:
```python
cc.map(layers=Layer('acadia_biodiversity', color='simpson_index')
```

Internally, the query should be
```SQL
SELECT * FROM acadiea_biodiversity ORDER BY simpson_index ASC
```

cc @makella

issue_title: Generated CFF table has undecodable subroutines
issue_body:
Checking the TTX dump of CFF tables generated by psautohint, the `Priviate.Subr` is full of undecoded CharStrings. I think this is because when we convert from CharStrings to bez format we “flatten” the subroutines, and when we convert back the old subroutines become unused and subsequently undecodable.

Not sure if we should do anything here as it probably only increases the file size. I considered just emptying `Priviate.Subr`, but this would break fonts where only a subset of the glyph are hinted (using the `-g` option). I don’t know if there is a way to detect unused subroutines and prune them.

issue_title: datastore: No way to exclude_from_indexes new properties on updated entities
issue_body:
When loading an `Entity` from the datastore and updating it, there is no way to exclude new attributes from being indexed, since the only public API for setting `exclude_from_indexes` is on the `Entity` constructor. To work around this, I am currently setting `entity._exclude_from_indexes = (...)` in my code, which is not a "public" API.

Example:

```python
client = google.cloud.datastore.Client()
key = client.key('SomeEntityKey')
entity = google.cloud.datastore.Entity(key, exclude_from_indexes=('foo', 'bar'))
entity['foo'] = 'foo'
print 'entity.exclude_from_indexes:', entity.exclude_from_indexes
client.put(entity)

entity2 = client.get(entity.key)
print 'entity2.exclude_from_indexes:', entity2.exclude_from_indexes
entity2['bar'] = 'bar'
client.put(entity2)
```

Output:

```
entity.exclude_from_indexes: frozenset(['foo', 'bar'])
entity2.exclude_from_indexes: frozenset([u'foo'])
```

This is actually "expected" based on how the Datastore works, however, in the code sample above, there should be some way for me to put the entity back, without copying it into a brand new `Entity`.


Tested using:

* Mac OS X 10.11.6
* Python 2.7.10
* The following Google packages:
```
gapic-google-cloud-datastore-v1==0.15.3
google-auth==1.0.1
google-auth-httplib2==0.0.2
google-cloud-core==0.24.1
google-cloud-datastore==1.0.0
google-cloud-storage==1.1.1
google-gax==0.15.13
google-resumable-media==0.0.2
googleapis-common-protos==1.5.2
proto-google-cloud-datastore-v1==0.90.4
```

issue_title: Monitoring: sending metrics in a non-blocking/buffered way
issue_body:
Hi there!

We're considering using this library to send metrics to Stackdriver monitoring.

I'm a bit concerned because the monitoring library seems to send metrics with a [synchronous HTTP connection](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/monitoring/google/cloud/monitoring/client.py#L66), thus coupling our services very tightly to Stackdriver and requiring a round-trip for each metric being sent.

In the the logging library, a [background thread](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/logging/google/cloud/logging/handlers/transports/background_thread.py#L17) seems to be used in order to buffer data locally before flushing it asynchronously.

Is there a built-in/suggested way to achieve a similar behaviour with metrics? I was considering subclassing the [Client](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/monitoring/google/cloud/monitoring/client.py#L52) class to do it.

Changing the first cell to be the word **feature** will make the GitHub markdown render the tables correctly.  Adding a colon (`:`) before the hyphens of the first column will make that addition be left-aligned.

:------------------ | --------- | -------------- | ----- | ------- | ------------- | ---------

issue_title: Handle Python version limitations more gracefully
issue_body:
Trying to import custom filters on Python 3.4.x and earlier fails silently because the import logic (see also: #23) tries to use `importlib.util.module_from_spec` which doesn't exist in versions before 3.5.

Dactyl should (at least) print a proper error/warning if you try to do this and maybe should allow you to skip over the custom filters or implement a v3.4-compatible import for them

issue_title: Duplicated questions in exams
issue_body:
### Observed behavior
When adding exercises to an exam from a topic that has repeated content nodes within it (like the top level KA channel when some of the elementary maths exercises have been imported). Each copy of the exercise will be rendered distinctly in the exam. This can lead to the same question being repeated in the exam.

### Expected behavior
Dedupe exercise nodes by content_id on exam creation.

### User-facing consequences
Confusing exams.

### Steps to reproduce
1. Import an exercise that is repeated across multiple topics (e.g. any of the exercises in the Khan Academy Counting topic).
2. Create an exam by selecting a topic that has that exercise in it more than once (e.g. the 'Math' topic in KA).
3. Preview the exam and see the same question repeated (but being drawn each time from a different copy of the exercise.

issue_title: Migrate from interfaces.juju.solutions to a GitHub repo
issue_body:
The layer index site works, but was originally intended to be an interim to finding a more permanent solution.   It has not, however, been improved or replaced.  It is also confusing for users in that it is separate from both the charm store and the code base.  Instead of maintaining an active index site & API, we should use a GitHub repo to manage the layer index.  This would allow us to use PRs for managing the index, giving more visibility to the community when layers are added, modified, or removed.

I think we should probably have a YAML file per record in the current database, and we might want to include some script or hook for updating a Markdown index (perhaps a subsection of the README.md) with clickable links to the repos referenced in the YAML files.

issue_title: Another fix in ProteinGrouper
Configuration stored as connection objects instead of on module singleton
issue_body:
Configurations are stored as global state on the `Configuration` module, example:

`Configuration.create(hdx_site='prod')`

I would prefer if Configurations were individually instantiated as a "connection" object. This would make application code easier to unit test and more decoupled. A good example is the AWS Boto API e.g.  
>>> from boto.s3.connection import S3Connection
>>> conn = S3Connection('<aws access key>', '<aws secret key>')
