issue_title: Add NI-SCOPE enum metadata
Leverage code generated methods in Session._get_error_description
issue_body:
Session class has a hand-coded function for error handling:

```
    def _get_error_description(self, error_code):
        new_error_code = ctypes_types.ViStatus_ctype(0)
        buffer_size = self.library.niDMM_GetError(self.vi, ctypes.byref(new_error_code), 0, None)
        assert (new_error_code.value == error_code)

        if (buffer_size > 0):
            '''
            Return code > 0 from first call to GetError represents the size of
            the description.  Call it again.
            Ignore incoming IVI error code and return description from the driver
            (trust that the IVI error code was properly stored in the session
            by the driver)
            '''
            error_code = ctypes_types.ViStatus_ctype(error_code)
            error_message = (ctypes_types.ViChar_ctype * buffer_size)()
            self.library.niDMM_GetError(self.vi, ctypes.byref(error_code), buffer_size, ctypes.cast(error_message, ctypes.POINTER(ctypes_types.ViChar_ctype)))
        else:
            '''
            Return code <= 0 from GetError indicates a problem.  This is expected
            when the session is invalid (IVI spec requires GetError to fail).
            Use GetErrorMessage instead.  It doesn't require a session.

            Call niDMM_GetErrorMessage, pass VI_NULL for the buffer in order to retrieve
            the length of the error message.
            '''
            error_code = buffer_size
            buffer_size = self.library.niDMM_GetErrorMessage(self.vi, error_code, 0, None)
            error_message = (ctypes_types.ViChar_ctype * buffer_size)()
            self.library.niDMM_GetErrorMessage(self.vi, error_code, buffer_size, ctypes.cast(error_message, ctypes.POINTER(ctypes_types.ViChar_ctype)))

        # TODO(marcoskirsch): By hardcoding encoding "ascii", internationalized strings will throw.
        #       Which encoding should we be using? https://docs.python.org/3/library/codecs.html#standard-encodings
        return new_error_code.value, error_message.value.decode("ascii")

```

We call directly into the library here, even though we have code-generated methods that wrap those things nicely. We should leverage them which will reduce the size of this hand-coded function, or even eliminate it.

issue_body:
```python
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/autobahn/asyncio/websocket.py", line 101, in connection_lost

  warnings.warn("unclosed transport %r" % self, ResourceWarning)
```

this is fixable with a quick single line, change WebSocketAdapterProtocol.connection_lost() at autobahn/asyncio/websocket.py:99 from this:
```python
    def connection_lost(self, exc):
        self._connectionLost(exc)
        self.transport = None
```

to this:
```python
    def connection_lost(self, exc):
        self._connectionLost(exc)
        self.transport.close()
        self.transport = None
```

**Describe the bug**
When you create a model tarball on a Mac, the system includes some binary dotfiles that cause predictions to crash when using the model.

**To Reproduce**
1. Create a tarball with `tar -czvf my_model.tar.gz config.json vocabulary/* weights.th`.
2. Make a prediction using the tarball you just created.

You can work around the error on a mac by creating your tarball as follows `COPYFILE_DISABLE=1; export COPYFILE_DISABLE; tar -czvf my_model.tar.gz config.json vocabulary/* weights.th`

issue_title: New nodes should be members, not proxies, on the etcd cluster
issue_body:
When you have a running deis cluster and AWS autoscaling replaces 1 node, etcd will come up as a proxy and not a member on the etcd cluster.

You can promote a proxy to a member but you have to manually do it https://github.com/coreos/etcd/blob/master/Documentation/proxy.md#promote-a-proxy-to-a-member-of-etcd-cluster.

You can also add the node as a member when it boots https://github.com/coreos/etcd/blob/master/Documentation/runtime-configuration.md#add-a-new-member but you have to set ETCD_INITIAL_CLUSTER and ETCD_INITIAL_CLUSTER_STATE instead of using a discovery url.

issue_title: etcd hardening and stress testing
issue_body:
We have recently seen several issues relating to etcd performance - specifically, several 503s and 501s. We need to spend time stress testing etcd to understand how to improve performance and reliability. Specifically, this should include:

* Testing on Docker 1.7.1 to see if the networking rewrite makes the Docker proxy more performant for etcdctl requests
* Re-evaluation of `etcdctl watch` usage (seemingly it causes far too much traffic because TTL updates are not filtered out - see coreos/etcd#952). Our use of polling loops and sleeps is clunky and issue prone.
* Rewrite of master election lock logic in bin/boot scripts in registry and store-gateway
* Support for Jenkins to record etcd performance statistics for runs so that regressions can be identified early

issue_title: Look into making globus-sdk[jwt] non-optional
issue_body:
Specifically, now that we're using `pyjwt` and (therefore) `cryptography`, we should consider making that dependency a normal requirement.
`globus-sdk[jwt]` would continue to exist as an empty extra for compatibility.

One of our key questions is whether or not we can trust AppVeyor testing to validate that Windows installs work. What version of Windows does AppVeyor run? If we can't trust it, how will we test for Windows? (If we can't trust it, doesn't that undermine all of our testing on that platform anyway?)

issue_title: Add support for 3-Legged OAuth for "Native App" clients to Globus Auth
issue_body:
Globus Auth is adding a concept of "Native App" clients which do a full 3-legged OAuth flow even though they do not have a client secret.
Doing this flow requires the use of a temporary PKCE secret to play the role of a client secret when getting an authorization code.

Although support for this flow in the CLI ( globus/globus-cli#16 ) is the first target, we should make sure that our bindings are sufficiently generic that they can be used for applications employing other flows.
In particular, some apps will be using an embedded browser to implement this flow, and supporting that as well as CLI use will probably cover the majority of our use cases.

After a discussion with @jaswilli, we reached the conclusion that there's little use for the state parameter passed through the OAuth flow, so we don't need to contend with the possibility of multiple simultaneous authentication flows. Handling one at a time will be sufficient for the foreseeable future.

@bd4 I think it's desirable for the SDK to encapsulate parts of this flow, as it has to generate a secret, send it, and then send a related secret to exchange the auth code for a token.
It's nice to think of a client for a given service as your way of "talking to" that service, so I think this should be built as a helper hanging off of the `AuthClient` object.

I'm putting together a proposed structure for this, even though the support for it in Globus Auth has not yet been released.

issue_title: List dependencies of a given package?
issue_body:
Trying to debug issue #2360 (maybe some dependencies were to blame, not conda) I **really** wanted to be able to run things like `conda info numpy=1.10.4-py27_1` but that does not work. So I had to rely on `conda info numpy | grep -A 15 1.10.4-py27_1`. I spent a fair amount of time in `conda help` and on the web, but could not find anything to specify a particular version (heck, even finding that `conda info package` was hard -- for a newbie to conda -- maybe you should add that info to http://conda.pydata.org/docs/faq.html#managing-packages?)

Anyway, an option to specify a particular version of the package would be extremely useful (or if such a thing already exist, telling about it into the conda help or online would be great).

issue_title: source activate is broken for zsh 4.3.10
issue_body:
I am using Anaconda on an older Linux box with zsh 4.3.10.  As of 9166defcbdc892aa7c466b9511fe74f36e0c7d5a the `source activate` exits my shell:

```zsh
$ source /path/to/anaconda/bin/activate
Error: activate must be sourced. Run 'source activate envname'
instead of 'activate envname'.
# shell exits
```
This happens because zsh 4.3.10 does not have the `ZSH_EVAL_CONTEXT` parameter yet and the [_conda_script_is_sourced](https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/9d759d8edeb86569c25f6eb82053f09581013a2a/conda/shell/bin/activate#L39-L53) then returns an incorrect result.

@kalefranz - can you please fix that function so it works with zsh 4.3.10?

If it is critical to enforce sourcing of the `activate` script, I feel it would be much simpler to just remove its executable permission.  It would be then impossible to run it otherwise than by sourcing and you could go away with all the complicated tests in `_conda_script_is_sourced`.

issue_title: Document new way to install and activate?
issue_body:
I see that the new way to set up conda is this:
```
. /opt/conda/etc/profile.d/conda.sh
conda activate base
```
* https://www.anaconda.com/blog/developer-blog/how-to-get-ready-for-the-release-of-conda-4-4/
* https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md#440-2017-12-20

I just now (June 1, 2018) installed Anaconda 5.2 with conda 4.5.4 and by default the installer still adds this to my `~/.bash_profile`, i.e. uses the old way to set things up:
```
# added by Anaconda3 5.2.0 installer
export PATH="/Users/deil/software/anaconda3/bin:$PATH"
```

Same with the docs (https://conda.io/docs/) - as far as I see they don't even mention the new way to set things up yet.

It's been about half a year since the conda 4.4 release. Time to update the docs and Anaconda installer to use the new way?

issue_title: Partial permissions errors in conda install leads to partially-installed package
issue_body:
Observed behavior: numba team had a docker image for building.  Docker image installed miniconda as root, but changed permissions on install folder to 777.  At some point, some install or update may have changed those permissions, leading to the lib/python2.7/site-packages/conda folder not being writable by the user.  However, the bin folder was writable.

conda update conda ran "successfully" - but updated only the activate scripts, not the conda folder.  Since the new conda 4.1.2 activates things with an extra argument, the old conda code was barfing.

We probably need to fail and roll back if any part of a package fails.

issue_title: Modelsim startup.do
issue_body:
I would like to do something like `vsim -gui -do "source startup.do" lib.design`, where startup.do will typically do stuff like adding signals to the waveform viewer. Is this possible with VUnit?

I am trying to use `VUnit.set_sim_option("modelsim.vsim_flags.gui", ["-do", "\"source startup.do\""])`, but I get a warning `Ignoring nested -do argument: "source startup.do"` and the script is not executed. I think the reason is that VUnit does `vsim -gui -do "do gui_load.do"`, and gui_load.do in turn calls vsim. Together that amounts to `vsim -gui -do "vsim -do \"source_startup.do\" lib.design"`, i.e. a "nested do"

I guess I could teach VUnit to run `vsim -gui -do "source startup.do" lib.design` with all the appropriate options, but then I cannot run vunit_run in the GUI any more, right?

issue_title: Flow with Vivado IP Integrator / External libraries
issue_body:
# Background
Recently I have been working with VUnit and Vivado IP Integrator (IPI) and running into library management difficulty. I have had success with using VUnit to map external libraries for Vivado IP and then simulating the IP - for example, simulating a single piece of IP (compiled into an external library) instantiated in a testbench (compiled into a VUnit managed library).

The primary difficulty is when plain HDL sources (that I would like to be managed by VUnit controlled library) are mixed into the Vivado IP Integrator block diagram.

In this configuration, IPI automatically creates a wrapper around the HDL file per-instance, and in the wrapper the HDL is instantiated by component instantiation with a default binding (no libraries, configurations, or entities are explicitly referenced). From my understanding of VHDL, the default binding implies it will search in the library that the component is compiled in, for an entity of the same name and port map as the component.

I bring this up because when initially planning on working with VUnit and Vivado IPI, I was hoping that I would be able to compile Vivado block diagram IP (e.g. all the generic Xilinx IP + project specific IP configurations) into a set of externally managed libraries, and then use VUnit to compile HDL sources into VUnit managed libraries. The typical flow would then be:

- Structure IPI block diagram in Vivado
- Run a (long running) process to generate IPI output products and compile Vivado IP for the block diagram
- Run VUnit simulations on portions of the block diagram, incrementally compiling changes to custom HDL files as development/debug continues.

My assumption has been I will reconfigure IP/block diagram infrequently, and will iterate on custom HDL designs frequently, so optimizing the compile/simulate loop for custom HDL is important.

Again, working with a single piece of IP at a time is straightforward since it can simply be instantiated by itself in a VUnit managed testbench. Working with a group of IP (in a block diagram hierarchy) or IP and HDL code is more difficult since portions of the block diagram itself must be instantiated - and have likely already been compiled together into various libraries as part of the Vivado IP compilation step.

I did investigate using VUnit to manage _all_ sources, but this is not feasible since some IP are encrypted and Vivado must be queried for proper compilation order and sources compiled separately (this is done in one of the examples in VUnit).

# Suggestions

- I believe this issue could be addressed, at least partially, by #179 - If VUnit supported a configuration as a top level simulation, then an explicit configuration could be used to bind the block diagram components for custom HDL entities explicitly to implementations in a VUnit managed library instead of the default binding that would occur to an entity in externally managed Vivado IP library. In this case, at least initially, the custom HDL source would be compiled identically into two different places: Vivado IP (external) library, and VUnit managed library for simulation only. After changes were made, the incremental compilation of VUnit managed library would get updated, and the version in the external library would not get updated or used.
- Allow VUnit to adopt/manage libraries that have already been created. I can imagine a lot of reasons why an approach like this could cause problems (e.g. with understanding dependencies). However, it seems like if a unit has already been successfully compiled into a library, there is no reason why VUnit could not take ownership of that library and simply recompile the file back into the same library (without wiping out the other contents).


If anyone has similar experiences or approaches that have worked for them, please let me know.

issue_title: ClientSession.request unexpectedly cancels task on timeout
issue_body:
## Long story short
`await client.get('https://httpbin.org/delay/10', timeout=.1)`
This call throws an exception on timeout, and it cancels the task it's running in.
This makes it hard to handle timeouts locally, e.g. by catching the timeout and returning an alternate value.

It would at least be worth documenting this behavior.

## Expected behaviour
I would have liked/expected the sample below to print:
`Caught exception!
api_req was able to handle timeout.`

## Actual behaviour
`Caught exception!
aiohttp cancelled us`


## Steps to reproduce
```python
import aiohttp
import asyncio

loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
client = aiohttp.ClientSession(loop=loop)

async def api_req():
    try:
        res = await client.get('https://httpbin.org/delay/10', timeout=.1)
    except asyncio.TimeoutError:
        print('Caught exception!')
        return False
    except:
        print('This should not happen.')
    return True

async def arun():
    done, not_done = await asyncio.wait([api_req()])
    done, = done
    try:
        assert done.result() == False
        print('api_req was able to handle timeout.')
    except asyncio.CancelledError:
        print('aiohttp cancelled us')
    except:
        print('This should not happen.')

loop.run_until_complete(arun())
client.close()
loop.close()
```

## Your environment
`python 3.6.2` on osx

issue_title: Should symlink option in nbextensions API fall back to copy?
issue_body:
`install_nbextension()` takes a `symlink=` boolean parameter, which instructs it to symlink the extension rather than copying it. In most cases this will fail on Windows, and the docstring says as much.

If symlink fails, should it automatically fall back on copying (the default without `symlink=True`)?

In favour: this is what most extensions probably want, and if we don't have a fallback, they have to have that fallback themselves.

Against: some extensions may rely on it actually making a symlink (e.g. symlink a directory into nbextensions, then change the contents of that directory). Automatic fallbacks make it harder to know what's really happening.

From ipython/ipython#8440

issue_title: Document how to customize cell code indentation
Error descriptions are not exposed on API level
vcf_to_hdf5 and variable length strings
issue_body: It appears that HDF5 uses a lot of space to store variable length strings, in the best case 32 bytes of overhead per string, plus the actual data. Also, the actual string data are not compressed. This can lead to very large file sizes for string columns like CHROM, REF, ALT etc.

issue_title: WHEEL file: Mention scikit-build in Generator field
issue_body:
Instead of 

```
Wheel-Version: 1.0
Generator: bdist_wheel (0.29.0)
Root-Is-Purelib: false
Tag: cp27-cp27mu-linux_x86_64
```

we could have

```
Wheel-Version: 1.0
Generator: skbuild.command.bdist_wheel (0.3.0)
Root-Is-Purelib: false
Tag: cp27-cp27mu-linux_x86_64
```

In section [file-contents](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0427/#file-contents), the PEP-0427 mentions the following:

```
[...]

8. Generator is the name and optionally the version of the software that produced the archive.

[...]
```

Implementation of `bdist_wheel` is in 

https://bitbucket.org/pypa/wheel/src/54ddbcc9cec25e1f4d111a142b8bfaa163130a61/wheel/bdist_wheel.py?at=default&fileviewer=file-view-default#bdist_wheel.py-260:265

```
[...]

    def write_wheelfile(self, wheelfile_base, generator='bdist_wheel (' + wheel_version + ')'):
        from email.message import Message
        msg = Message()
        msg['Wheel-Version'] = '1.0'  # of the spec
        msg['Generator'] = generator
        msg['Root-Is-Purelib'] = str(self.root_is_pure).lower()
[...]
```

Otherwise, here is what other tools are doing:
- https://github.com/takluyver/flit/blob/master/flit/wheel.py#L21

```
[...]
wheel_file_template = """\
Wheel-Version: 1.0
Generator: flit {version}
Root-Is-Purelib: true
""".format(version=__version__)
[...]
```
-  https://bitbucket.org/dholth/enscons/src/

```
???
```

```
???

issue_title: client.py argument checking
issue_body:
Hi Peter,

When I am working on adding the pie support for locate operation, I do notice that there are some issues about the arguments checking in client.py. It's too strict, I think. For example, for get() operation,  from the spec, uid can be ignored  while it must be a string in the codes. This means that using the pie interface, I can not issue an get request without any uid? 

Should we refactor this some time? I think your vision is to put all the argument validation inside the payload module in the future?

I've written a [simple module -> submodule example](https://gist.github.com/vierja/d0879ff294a784758730fa92a4973005) with and without `_enter_variable_scope` and I've got different graphs in Tensorboard.

issue_title: RelationBase.conversation() returns an arbitrary SERVICE or UNIT scoped conversation
issue_body:
A service can be related to another service multiple times. To cope with this, conversations are namespaced using the relation id (the only key), and we end up with one SERVICE scoped conversation per relation

The RelationBase.conversation() classmethod only has a single scope parameter, and can only return one of the several SERVICE scoped conversations. The rest are inaccessible.

The same issue also occurs for UNIT scoped conversations.

issue_title: Support for MS Edge is broken
issue_body:
MS Edge crashes when clearing cookies:
https://developer.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-edge/platform/issues/5751773/

This always happens on browser start in prepare_browser:
https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest-splinter/blob/c4cf38683ee8eff0a6cecc0fb21c118d5cf1d34a/pytest_splinter/plugin.py#L411

The infinite retry behavior in prepare_browser (see #71, #72, #73) hides this error and restarts the browser infinitely.

issue_title: Fix net fdb test cases
We need to publish and implement a policy that dictates how test dependencies are to be handled
issue_body:
Should test dependencies (e.g. mock, responses, pytest) be in requirements.txt?
Should there be a switch that allows an installer to choose whether or not they want test dependencies?  If yes, then how should the switch be implemented?
What should it be set to by default?

I suppose @jlongstaf, @pjbreaux, @swormke, and I should at least know what policy we decide on, so we can correctly implement in future repositories.

I'm tentatively assigning this to @richbrowne since he's the packaging czar.  (As always I believe we should feel free to reassign issues willy-nilly.)

issue_title: Unit test coverage at 100% for multi_device/cluster
issue_body: The unit tests for f5/multi_device/device_group.py f5/multi_device/trust_domain.py and f5/multi_device/cluster/__init__.py should be at 100% line coverage. This is currently being accomplished by a combination of unit and functional tests.

issue_title: Add net/self-allow endpoint
Standarize testing patterns
issue_body:
As per comment in PR #1040, I would like us to discuss the established patterns for testing in this library, these will include:

- What we test in unit and functional tests, mainly the scope of those tests.
- The format of test fixtures, e.g out with teardown() in with yield
- Factory classes to reduce the amount of repetitive code.
- When not to test certain elements, e.g due to specifics of BIGIP operation, or limitations, whether its 
feasible to work around those.
- Adherence to code coverage, e.g. no 100% code coverage (whenever possible) means no PR approval

@caphrim007 @jasonrahm @zancas @pjbreaux

issue_title: Apache vhost selection with install
issue_body:
As reported by @nils-ohlmeier, when you use the Apache plugin with the `install` verb, it defaults to installing the certificate to all found vhosts. This is true even if the certificate is only valid for a subset of these vhosts.

Apache should parse the certificate and only offer to install it for vhosts whose domain names are in the certificate or whose domain names are unknown.

issue_title: Best practice/native support for implicit variables in config, lazy eval
issue_body:
In my past few months of sacred usage, something that I have often encountered and wished I had a cleaner way to express are implicit dependent variables within my config. 

I think this is best explained by way of example - for instance, suppose you have a model config specifying the topology of your model and wish to store the receptive field of the resulting model. For a moderately complicated model/parameterization the only reasonable way to calculate the receptive field is just to build the model and compare the input and output shapes - an expensive operation that I'm going to do in a sec anyway when I start the experiment. Thus lazy evaluation for the receptive field property would be ideal. 

Any recommendations on best practice for accomplishing this? All I can think of now is to store the receptive field as a scalar metric. 

Another example - suppose you have different datasets, sampled at different rates, which can be selected from in your data config. You wish to store the sampling rate of the selected dataset in the config along with the dataset selection itself. Existing config dependency mechanisms work fine for this: 

```
@experiment.config
def cfg():
    data = {
        dataset: 'foo',
        sample_rate: None
    }
    data['sample_rate'] = fetch_sample_rate(data)
```

However there is no nice way to prevent the user from overriding `data['sample_rate']` (you could raise an exception in `fetch_sample_rate`).  I'm considering a underscore prefix notation to indicate implicit dependent variables (`'_sample_rate'`) which would help clarify, but still not ideal. 

Having hopefully made the case that this would be a nice feature, let me try to make some specific proposals:

- Add explicit support for 'implicit dependent variables', call them 'annotations' maybe. I propose adopting an underscore prefix notation to indicate which config entries are annotations, allowing the config engine to raise an error if a user tries to directly set the value of an annotation. 
- Add support for lazy evaluation of annotations. That is, allow the value of annotations to be set after an experiment has started. 

What do you think?

issue_title: [Documentation] Improve doc for Essential Methods
issue_body:
Since implementing a Filesystem actually requires a restricted set of methods, they should be well documented to state the expected behaviour. Currently, the following pieces of information are missing in the documentation:

## open()
* **Parameters**:
  - `encoding` states it defaults to `utf-9`
  - `**options` is not listed
  - the link to the [stdllib error-handler](https://docs.python.org/3/library/codecs.html#error-handlers) does not render well on RTD, why not use the ``` `name <link>`_ ``` format in the docstrings ?

## openbin()
* **Errors not listed**: 
  - raises `ValueError` when explictly given a text mode ('wt', 'rt', 'at'...) or an invalid mode ('h', 'z', ...)
  - raises `FileExpected` when path goes to a directory
  - raises `FileExists` when mode is 'x' or 'xb'
  - raises `ResourceNotFound` when mode is 'r' or 'rb'
* **Note**: 
  - maybe write somewhere that 'r', 'w', 'x', and 'a' are automatically coerced to their binary counterpart (this is a bit counterintuitive when compared with Python `open`)

## remove()
* **Error issues**:
  - is listed to raise `FileExpected` when given a directory whereas the test module expects `InvalidResource` to be raised (`FileExpected` makes more sense I think)

## removedir()
* **Errors not listed**:
  - raises `RemoveRootError` when attempting to remove the root of the fs

* **Error issues**:
  - is listed to raise `ParentDirectoryMissing` when one of the ancestors in the given path does not exist but this error does not exist

## setinfo()
* **Errors not listed**:
  - raises `ResourceNotFound` when the path does not exist

issue_title: When `pivot` is called with only a function but no values column, pass a list of rows to the function
Repr of djmoney.money.Money rounded down
issue_body:
Sorry if this has already been brought up, but I couldn't see anything obvious when searching for repr.

Within a console, if I do the following I get confusing results:

```py
In [63]: m
Out[63]: 9 GBP

In [64]: type(m)
Out[64]: djmoney.money.Money

In [65]: vars(m)
Out[65]: 
{'_constructor_args': ((), {'amount': Decimal('9.75'), 'currency': u'GBP'}),
 'amount': Decimal('9.75'),
 'currency': GBP}
```

However, constructing a `moneyed.Money` result produces the following:

```py
In [67]: m2
Out[67]: <Money: 9.75 GBP>

In [68]: vars(m2)
Out[68]: {'amount': Decimal('9.75'), 'currency': GBP}

In [70]: type(m2)
Out[70]: moneyed.classes.Money
```
It seems like the [py-moneyed team took the decision to remove the rounding down in 2015](https://github.com/limist/py-moneyed/pull/67) - is there a reason you've chosen not to? Looking at your implementation would suggest that you copied the code verbatim when constructing your overloaded version. Should I be exclusively using `djmoney.money.Money` over `moneyed.Money`?

issue_title: Author of comment no longer displayed
issue_body:
Observed using v2.1.0 (on CentOS 6 and 7). But likely still present in the current master (see link below).

Since a few weeks (not sure how long), bin/bugzilla is no longer showing author of comments when querying with '--outputformat "%{comments}"':

```
$ bugzilla --debug query --bug_id 1433987 --outputformat "%{comments}"
[13:11:05] DEBUG (bugzilla:1043) Launched with command line: /usr/bin/bugzilla --debug query --bug_id 1433987 --outputformat %{comments}
[13:11:05] INFO (bugzilla:1046) Connecting to https://bugzilla.redhat.com/xmlrpc.cgi
[13:11:05] DEBUG (base:313) Using tokenfile=/home/guess/.cache/python-bugzilla/bugzillatoken
[13:11:05] DEBUG (base:442) Using cookiefile=/home/guess/.bugzillacookies
[13:11:06] DEBUG (base:560) Bugzilla version string: 4.4.12074.2
[13:11:06] INFO (base:330) Using RHBugzilla for URL containing bugzilla.redhat.com
[13:11:06] DEBUG (base:1211) Calling Bug.search with: {'include_fields': ['comments', 'id'], 'id': ['1433987']}
[13:11:07] DEBUG (base:1227) Query returned 1 bugs
[13:11:07] DEBUG (bug:41) Bug(['comments', 'creation_time', 'id'])

* 20170320T13:55:36 - :
A vulnerability found in the NTP server makes it possible for an authenticated remote user to crash ntpd via a malformed mode configuration directive.
...
```

But it still shows up when using '--outputformat "%{longdescs}"':
```
$ bugzilla --debug query --bug_id 1433987 --outputformat "%{longdescs}"
[13:13:59] DEBUG (bugzilla:1043) Launched with command line: /usr/bin/bugzilla --debug query --bug_id 1433987 --outputformat %{longdescs}
[13:13:59] INFO (bugzilla:1046) Connecting to https://bugzilla.redhat.com/xmlrpc.cgi

[13:14:01] DEBUG (base:1227) Query returned 1 bugs

I've figured the attribute name may have changed from "creator" to "author" which can be easily fixed [here](https://github.com/python-bugzilla/python-bugzilla/blob/49f7d78aaf36c9cc9e3a30a9f492ae4ed364d06e/bin/bugzilla#L689).

issue_title: duplicate an AreaDefinition object with coarser/finer definition
issue_body:
Add a feature for the AreaDefinition class (https://pyresample.readthedocs.org/en/latest/geo_def.html#areadefinition). I would need a class method that creates a new AreaDef object from an existing one. The new AreaDef has the same projection and coordinate extent, but the grid_spacing (equivalently the number of gridpoints x_size and y_size) is scaled by a factor provided by the user. This would return a new AreaDef that matches exactly the existing one, but with twice (thrice, four times as much) the original resolution.

Example:
area_def = geometry.AreaDefinition(area_id, name, proj_id, proj_dict, x_size,y_size, area_extent)
new_area_def = area_def.finer_grid_spacing(3,2)
# new_aera_def now has three times as much cells in the x axis, and twice in the y axis. If only 1 value is given, the same is used for x and y.

issue_title: Add dashboard retrieval to CI client
Support for URLs in input requirements.txt files
issue_body:
Hi,

This is a feature request.

Lets start by stating that, following the requirements file format spec, URLs to tarballs are part supported:
https://pip.readthedocs.io/en/1.1/requirements.html#requirements-file-format

Now, the `pkg_resources.parse_requirments` function used by `safety` does not support them:
https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/blob/master/pkg_resources/__init__.py#L2850
It raises a `RequirementParseError: Invalid requirement, parse error`.

I had a look at how they handle this in `pip`, and it's ugly:
https://github.com/pypa/pip/blob/master/pip/req/req_set.py#L690

`pip-tools` does not support them (it actually crashes in a bad way if you try so: https://github.com/nvie/pip-tools/issues/416)

By the way, URLs to tarball specified as editable requirements (with `-e`) work fine: curiously `pkg_resources.parse_requirments` handle them perfectly well.

What do you think ? Should `safety` handle them ?

### Bug description
This issue continues discussion started in #221 about barcode generation.

issue_title: Trainer does not change TF Variables?
issue_body:
This is probably more an issue of me understanding TFLearn. However, I noticed that training a network using tflearn.trainer does not change the values of the variables even though a decrease in the loss is reported. A simple toy example with a single scalar weight being trained is here:

```
import tensorflow as tf
import tflearn
import numpy as np

sess = tf.InteractiveSession()

input_var = tf.placeholder(dtype=tf.float32, shape=(None, 1))
inp      = tflearn.input_data(placeholder=input_var, name='Input_layer')
enc      = tflearn.fully_connected(inp, n_units=1, activation='ReLU', name='Encoding_1')
loss     = tf.reduce_mean((enc - 3*inp)**2)

sess.run(tf.initialize_all_variables())

print(sess.run(tf.trainable_variables()))

optimizer = tf.train.AdamOptimizer(1e-3)
trainop   = tflearn.TrainOp(loss=loss,
                            optimizer=optimizer,
                            metric=None,
                            batch_size=100)

trainer = tflearn.Trainer(train_ops=trainop)

trainer.fit(feed_dicts={input_var : np.random.randn(100000,1).astype(np.float32)},
            show_metric=True,
            n_epoch=2, run_id='beta')

print(sess.run(tf.trainable_variables()))
```
Before and after training the weights are the same despite a decline in the regression loss (as reported during training)! This is a completely counter-intuitive behaviour for me. Could somebody explain to me what is going on? Thanks!

issue_title: [PATCH] >=0.22.0: Bug in acme.Client.__init__ breaks acme.Client.register
issue_labels: [{'id': 277074172, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20acme', 'name': 'area: acme', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 853314368, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20encoding/type%20error', 'name': 'area: encoding/type error', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 146954787, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/bug', 'name': 'bug', 'color': 'fc2929', 'default': True}]
issue_body:
Hi!

There is a bug in `acme.Client.__init__`. When called with `net=None`, `self.net` is first initialited with a new instance of `ClientNetwork` but then `net` (rather than `self.net`) is pass to `ClientBase.__init__` which sets `self.net` back to None. The breaking code was introduced in 9baf75d6c887a9b69bd92c7bffe585f091c9b08e and is this:
```
class ClientBase(object):  # pylint: disable=too-many-instance-attributes
    [..]
    def __init__(self, directory, net, acme_version):
        [..]
        self.net = net
[..]
class Client(ClientBase):
    [..]
    def __init__(self, directory, key, alg=jose.RS256, verify_ssl=True,
                 net=None):
        [..]
        self.net = ClientNetwork(key, alg=alg, verify_ssl=verify_ssl) if net is None else net
        [..]
        super(Client, self).__init__(directory=directory,
            net=net, acme_version=1)
```
As a result, calling `.register(...)` on the client will raise `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'post'` which breaks wile, see https://github.com/costela/wile/issues/34.

One way to fix the issue is to change `net=net` to `self.net=net` in the line using `super`. I could provide a pull request to do that if that's the way you want to patch it.

What do you think?

issue_title: Cross-subkernel %get and %put
issue_body:
Right now if we need to exchange data between two subkernels, it would be done through

```
subkernel A -> SoS -> subkernel B
```

We should perhaps add more data exchange capacities such as

1. Language A to Language A, because in this case there are usually faster and loseless methods and passing through SoS would lead to unnecessary loss in data and efficiency.

2. Language A to Language B directly. For example, `R` has its own package to read `SAS` dataset so it would be better to use it instead of transferring data from `SAS` to `SoS` and then to `R`.

issue_title: Stale partition URL after delete/create partition
issue_labels: [{'id': 429586299, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/zhmcclient/python-zhmcclient/labels/area:%20code', 'name': 'area: code', 'color': '207de5', 'default': False}, {'id': 429588767, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/zhmcclient/python-zhmcclient/labels/resolution:%20fixed', 'name': 'resolution: fixed', 'color': 'cccccc', 'default': False}, {'id': 429587708, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/zhmcclient/python-zhmcclient/labels/type:%20bug', 'name': 'type: bug', 'color': '84b6eb', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
### Actual behavior

If a "Create NIC" operation is issued on a partition that was just deleted and created again, it fails with HTTP status 404, reason 1, message "Not found or not authorized".

The sequence of HMC operations for reproducing the failure is:

- Logon
- List CPCs (and pick one)
- List Partitions (filter by name)
- Delete Partition
- Create Partition
- List partitions (filter by name)
- Create NIC on the resulting partition -> fails with 404,1

The WS API log on the HMC shows that the Create NIC operation in its target URL uses the partition ID of the partition resource that was deleted, when it should have used the newly created partition resource.

### Expected behavior

Use the partition ID of newly created partition, instead.

### Execution environment

* zhmcclient version: 0.12.0
* Operating system (type+version):
* HMC version: 2.13.1
* CPC version: 2.13.1

issue_title: Fix CLI description of `stack rm --force` and prompt
issue_body:
The CLI help text and interactive prompt for `--force` is misleading, as a beta user thought it was similar to `rm -f`. (I made the same mistake myself.)

## Current 

```
  -f, --force   By default, removal of a stack with resources will be rejected; this forces it
```

```
$ pulumi stack rm --force
This will permanently remove the 'webserver-donna' stack!
Please confirm that this is what you'd like to do by typing ("webserver-donna"): 
```

## Proposal

For flag: consider `--orphan`. (If necessary, keep `--force` as an alias.)

For help content:

```
  -f, --force   Forces removal of a stack, potentially orphaning resources. 
```

Interactive prompt:

```
$ pulumi stack rm --force
Warning: stack 'webserver-donna' contains 4 resources. 
This operation will permanently remove 'webserver-donna', potentially orphaning these resources.
Please confirm that this is what you'd like to do by typing ("webserver-donna"): 
```

Seems to be expecting to receive escaped unicode. However in my environment that doesn't appear to be case. My workaround:

```
                if six.PY3:
                    try:
                        # workaround to get unicode text out rather than escaped text
                        body = body.encode('ascii').decode('unicode_escape')
                    except UnicodeEncodeError:

issue_title: Upgrading to sympl 0.4.0
issue_body:
sympl 0.4.0 will bring a lot of changes which are incompatible with climt as it currently is. We will need to

* [ ] Change code so that 'x', 'y' wildcards are no longer used anywhere. This will require registering dimension names in `get_default_state()` which will be used to create the `*_properties` during run time.
* [ ] Get rid of all irrelevant code in `ArrayHandler`.
* [ ] Change all components to use the `array_call` interface
* [ ] New TimeSteppers won't work in convection schemes. Need to write new ones.
* [ ] If possible, figure out and move most memory allocation to 'c' format

I will keep updating this list as and when I figure out new requirements.

issue_title: [V3 Unload/Reload] edge cases in bot.unload_extension
issue_body:
### The issue

Right now, `bot.unload_extension` will break unload and reload if any cogs exist on the bot where `Cog.__module__` is `None` 

While this is definitely an edge case, I think it is at least worth considering just filtering out any case where there isn't a module, and trust that in that case, the user of created that situation can handle any required cleanup.

### The use case

Adding a cog through eval to test a specific behavior temporarily, without creating an entire module for adding through standard means.

(yes, this is an extreme edge case, but it's how I've run into it, so even a "we won't support this" is a fine answer if that's the decision)

issue_title: Rename make_dataset argument
issue_body:
Should try to select names that are consistent within the codebase. This would improve maintainability for future developers.
For example, the "extent" argument of datacube.model.utils.make_dataset and the "extent" property of a datacube.model.Dataset object refer to different concepts. One intends the maximal rectangular extent of the array (aka. "bounds"), and the other intends the extent of a tighter envelope which excludes parts of the array that only contain nodata values (aka. "valid_data").
Similarly, e.g. xarray.Dataset versus postgres agdc.dataset table versus datacube.model.Dataset..

issue_body:
> Would it be worthwhile to add subprojects under python-trio for various popular APIs
> … gain the ability to run in "trio mode", 

Not to suggest refactoring this all over again, but I've been thinking about #105 and #171. I think the `_file_io` stuff is actually pretty re-usable, even for asyncio (definitely much less scary than aiofiles anyway).

What do you think about ripping all of this back out, then making a `python-trio/threaded-file-io` or similar that supports creating both asyncio and trio threads (which I think is a fourth variant of `*-libs-on-*`).

issue_title: Make all tests runnable with `processes` nosetest option
issue_body:
Developers should be able to run all of the nosetests with the `processes` option to speed things up. This does not work with some of the tests, because they expect to have something setup which is done when they are run sequentially. We should identify the pieces that need to be run sequentially and require that explicitly using the `nose.with_setup` decorator, so that developers can call `nosetests --processes=N` to speed things up. For example, on my machine running

```
nosetests -v test_aalst_patterns test_data test_checkpointing --processes=8
```
instead of
```
nosetests -v test_aalst_patterns test_data test_checkpointing
```
results in a speedup from 20 sec -> 8 sec.

issue_title: Establish benchmark workflows
issue_body: We should establish benchmark workflows that we can use to stress test Parsl in order to compare different Parsl versions. Preferably these would be run before each release, so that the impact of new features on performance can be documented and understood.

issue_title: [Question] Will Kubernetes client python supports to create helm resources?
issue_body: There are some use cases for communicating with Helm server, I think it is better if Python client can do that.

issue_title: Adapt the redundant free scheme to only compare if blocking rule covers pairs n times
issue_body:
It would be very useful to have a predicate rule like "shares 3 tokens"

I wonder if we could adapt the redundant-free scheme to only compare records if a record pair was covered N times. If so, then we could set up this type of predicate rule without adding to algorithm complexity.

issue_title: Remove dependence upon PYTHONHASHSEED
issue_body: The result from the search for predicates depends upon the initial order of the set of predicates.  That order depends upon the value of `PYTHONHASHSEED` since it's using a set. I've not thoroughly looked for other places where this causes a dependency, but it'd be nice to support fully reproducible runs with just `random.seed` and `numpy.random.seed`.

issue_title: Message attributes are not delivered when publishing a message on a SNS topic and receiving it from an SQS queue
issue_body:
#Hi,

When following the scenario:

---------------------------------------
`# CREATE SNS TOPIC AND SQS QUEUE`
`aws --endpoint-url=http://localhost:4576 --region us-east-1 sqs create-queue --queue-name queue1`
`aws --endpoint-url=http://localhost:4575 --region us-east-1 sns create-topic --name topic1`

`# EXTRACT 'QueueArn' ATTRIBUTE FOR SQS QUEUE`
`aws --endpoint-url=http://localhost:4576 --region us-east-1 sqs get-queue-attributes --queue-url $QUEUE_URL # extract arn of queue`

`# SUBSCRIBE SQS QUEUE ON TOPIC`
`aws --endpoint-url=http://localhost:4575 --region us-east-1 sns subscribe --topic-arn $TOPIC_ARN --protocol sqs --notification-endpoint $QUEUE_ARN`

`# PUBLISH MESSAGE`
`aws --endpoint-url=http://localhost:4575 --region us-east-1 sns publish --message text --message-attributes '{"an-attribute-key": {"DataType": "string","StringValue": "an-attribute-value"}}' --topic-arn $TOPIC_ARN`

`# RECEIVE MESSAGE`
`aws --endpoint-url=http://localhost:4576 --region us-east-1 sqs receive-message --queue-url $QUEUE_URL`

---------------------------------------

issue_title: GRU equations/python
issue_body:
Should R[zrh] be treated the same as W[zrh] values and be transposed before use? That would match the CNTK implementation and be a more consistent treatment of the W and R values. 

Current equations are
    zt = f(Xt*(Wz^T) + Ht-1*Rz + Wbz + Rbz)
    rt = f(Xt*(Wr^T) + Ht-1*Rr + Wbr + Rbr)
    ht = g(Xt*(Wh^T) + (rt (.) Ht-1)*Rh + Rbh + Wbh) # default, when linear_before_reset = 0
    ht = g(Xt*(Wh^T) + (rt (.) (Ht-1*Rh + Rbh) + Wbh) # when linear_before_reset != 0
    Ht = (1 - zt) (.) ht + zt (.) Ht-1

current python from GRU_helper.py
  z = self.f(np.dot(self.X, np.transpose(w_z)) + np.dot(self.H_0, r_z) + w_bz + r_bz)
  r = self.f(np.dot(self.X, np.transpose(w_r)) + np.dot(self.H_0, r_r) + w_br + r_br)
  h_default = self.g(np.dot(self.X, np.transpose(w_h)) + np.dot(r * self.H_0, r_h) + w_bh + r_bh)
  h_linear = self.g(np.dot(self.X, np.transpose(w_h)) + r * (np.dot(self.H_0, r_h) + r_bh) + w_bh)

python with transpose of R[zrh] that produces same results as CNTK
  z = self.f(np.dot(row, np.transpose(w_z)) + np.dot(self.H_0, np.transpose(r_z)) + w_bz + r_bz)
  r = self.f(np.dot(row, np.transpose(w_r)) + np.dot(self.H_0, np.transpose(r_r)) + w_br + r_br)
  h_default = self.g(np.dot(row, np.transpose(w_h)) + np.dot(r * self.H_0, np.transpose(r_h)) + w_bh + r_bh)
  h_linear = self.g(np.dot(row, np.transpose(w_h)) + r * (np.dot(self.H_0, np.transpose(r_h)) + r_bh) + w_bh)

issue_title: proposal: optimization pass  converting Shape() + inferred static shape into initializers
issue_body: I use current version of pytorch/onnx to export an resnet50 model. It generates a Shape() operator whose result can be inferred statically given fixed size of input to the network. Does it make sense to implement optimization passes to (1) convert Shape +inferred shape into static initializer for the input of the downstream next operator? (2) perform [constant folding](https://www.google.com/search?q=constant+folding&ie=utf-8&oe=utf-8&client=firefox-b-1-ab) to operators with only constant inputs such as Slice, Squeeze, Unsqueeze, Concat operators in my case?

issue_title: make distributed training transparent to users
issue_body:
Now we have initial support for distributed training in `tl.distributed`, and an [example](https://github.com/tensorlayer/tensorlayer/blob/master/example/tutorial_mnist_distributed.py) of how to use it.

I think we can do even better, by hiding all the details in `tl.utils.fit`, so that all the tutorials can be run as distributed training without changing a single byte.

This can be achieved by making `tl.utils.fit ` aware of the `TF_CONFIG` variable.

issue_title: If opened w/o encryption, following calls must clear default flag
issue_body:
If you open the connection with encryption disabled (which is not the default), then all following calls to `send_recv` must have encrypt=False (also not the default), or else you'll get this error:

```
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/ckcc/client.py", line 99, in send_recv
    msg = self.encrypt_request(msg)
TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not callable
```

Kinda an API limitation. Maybe `send_recv()` should check, or not... idk.

issue_title: 为 visualMap 新增 pieces 参数
issue_body:
为 visualMap 组件新增了 pieces 参数，该参数仅在 is_piecewise 为 True 时生效。适用于所有支持 visualMap 组件的图形种类，

```
:param pieces:
        自定义『分段式视觉映射组件（visualMapPiecewise）』的每一段的范围，
        以及每一段的文字，以及每一段的特别的样式。例如：
        pieces: [
            {min: 1500}, // 不指定 max，表示 max 为无限大（Infinity）。
            {min: 900, max: 1500},
            {min: 310, max: 1000},
            {min: 200, max: 300},
            {min: 10, max: 200, label: '10 到 200（自定义label）'},
            {value: 123, label: '123（自定义特殊颜色）', color: 'grey'}, // 表示 value 等于 123 的情况。
            {max: 5}     // 不指定 min，表示 min 为无限大（-Infinity）。
        ]
```
以 Map 为例

代码
```
def test_visualmap_pieces():
    value = [155, 10, 66, 78]
    attr = ["福建", "山东", "北京", "上海"]
    map = Map("全国地图示例", width=1200, height=600)
    map.add("", attr, value, maptype='china',
            is_visualmap=True, is_piecewise=True,
            visual_text_color="#000",
            visual_range_text=["", ""],
            pieces=[
                {
                    "max": 160,
                    "min": 70,
                    "label": "高数值"
                },
                {
                    "max": 69,
                    "min": 0,
                    "label": "低数值"
                },
            ])
    map.render()
```

效果图

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19553554/36943053-22748c70-1fbd-11e8-86dc-ac3d48f5c4e3.png)

可以看到，VisualMap 的标签已经被自定义了
